entries.description: Operating a Motorboat While Intoxicated (BUI Baseline)—Engaging in the operation or handling of a motorboat or vessel on state public waters while under the influence of an intoxicant, a restricted controlled substance, or with a prohibited blood alcohol concentration of 0.08 or more.

Operating a Motorboat While Intoxicated (Recidivist Tier)—Operating a motorboat or vessel on state public waters while under the influence of an intoxicant, a restricted controlled substance, or with a prohibited BAC of 0.08 or more, carrying one prior qualified OWI or BUI conviction within a 7-year tracking window.

Aggravated Criminal BUI Causing Injury—Operating a motorboat or vessel on state public waters while under the influence of an intoxicant, a restricted controlled substance, or with a prohibited BAC of 0.08 or more, causing great bodily harm or injury to another person.

Refusal to Submit to Chemical Testing (Boating)—Refusing a law enforcement officer or DNR warden's lawful request for a breath, blood, or urine sample under a boating under the influence (BUI) or fatal vessel crash investigation.

Boating Under the Influence (Municipal Adoption)—Operating a motorboat, personal watercraft, or vessel on navigable waters within city/county boundaries while under the influence of an intoxicant or with a BAC of 0.08 or more.
entries.statuteTitle: Milwaukee Code of Ordinances (Adopting Navigable Water Regulations)

entries.description: Theft of Movable Property (Felony Baseline)—Intentionally taking, carrying away, using, transferring, concealing, or retaining possession of movable property of another without consent and with intent to deprive the owner permanently of possession, where the value of the property exceeds $2,500 but does not exceed $5,000.

High-Tier Felony Property Theft—Intentionally taking, carrying away, transferring, or concealing property of another without consent, valued above $5,000 up to and exceeding $100,000, to permanently deprive the owner.

Retail Theft (Misdemeanor Baseline)—Concealing or taking possession of merchant inventory, altering price tags, or transferring containers to deprive a merchant permanently of the full purchase value, where the merchandise value does not exceed $2,500.

Aggravated Felony Retail Theft—Concealing, taking, or altering price tags of merchant merchandise valued above $2,500, or acting in concert with a theft ring or using theft-detection shielding devices to bypass merchant security parameters.

Armed Burglary—Violating the perimeters of any building or dwelling without consent and with intent to steal or commit a felony, while armed with a dangerous weapon, or arming oneself with a dangerous weapon while inside the structure, or executing a physical battery on a lawful occupant.

Attempted Burglary to a Dwelling—Performing acts demonstrating unequivocally an intent to enter a building or dwelling without consent to commit a theft or felony, which are interrupted or fall short of entry.
Attempted Petty Theft—Acts demonstrating unequivocally an intent to steal property or conceal merchandise under $2,500 that are prevented or fall short of completion.
Petty Shoplifting (Municipal Forfeiture)—Concealing or taking retail merchandise without consent inside city borders where the value matches low-tier local thresholds.
entries.statuteTitle: Milwaukee Code of Ordinances (Adopting State Property Offenses)

entries.description: Retail Theft Civil Liability Demand—The statutory mechanism enabling retail merchants to sue or demand civil exemplary damages and retail restitution directly from any adult or emancipated minor who commits retail theft under § 943.50.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Civil Summary Procedure / Merchant Tort)

entries.description: Property Restitution Judgment Lien—The conversion of a criminal court restitution order into an enforceable civil judgment lien against all real property owned by the defendant in the county of docketing, executing financial holds on property transfers.

Attempted Identity Theft—Performing acts demonstrating unequivocally an intent to use or possess another's personal identifying information without consent to obtain credit, money, or services, which are prevented or fall short of completion.

entries.subcategory: Bribery of a Public Servant / Official
entries.description: Bribery of Public Officers or Employees—Transferring, offering, or promising to a public officer or public employee any property or personal advantage with intent to influence their vote, opinion, judgment, or official action; or the acceptance of such benefits by an official.

Theft by Virtue of Employment (Embezzlement Baseline)—By virtue of office, business, or employment, intentionally having possession or custody of movable property or funds of another, and intentionally converting or misallocating such property without the owner's consent, where the value does not exceed $2,500.

Theft by Virtue of Employment (Aggravated Felony Tiers)—Intentionally converting or misallocating funds or property held in active fiduciary custody by virtue of office or employment without consent, where the aggregate stolen value exceeds $2,500.

Perjury—Willfully making a false material statement under oath or affirmation in a matter, cause, action, or proceeding before a court, judge, magistrate, referee, or administrative body authorized to administer oaths.

Subornation of Perjury—Intentionally procuring, instigating, commanding, or inducing another person to commit perjury by giving false material testimony under oath in a judicial or administrative proceeding.

False Statements (Unsworn Falsification)—Making a false written statement which the actor does not believe to be true, on or in a form bearing notice authorized by law that false statements made therein are punishable, with intent to mislead a public servant in the performance of their duty.

Fraudulent Writings (Corporate Ledger Falsification)—Any person who, with intent to injure or defraud, falsifies, alters, or destroys any corporate books, records, accounts, financial statements, or balance sheets belonging to a business corporation or commercial enterprise.

Counterfeiting and Trademark Infringement—Intentionally manufacturing, counterfeiting, replicating, or uttering any registered trademark, commercial label, or financial designator of another without authorization and with intent to pass goods off as authentic.

Fraudulent Deceptive Marketing—Making, publishing, or circulating any statement, representation, or advertisement regarding real estate, insurance, merchandise, or services which contains any assertion, representation, or statement which is untrue, deceptive, or misleading with intent to induce public commerce.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Marketing and Trade Practices Code)

entries.description: Fraudulent Use of Financial Transaction Card—Using a financial transaction card or card security codes for the purpose of obtaining money, goods, or services with intent to defraud and knowing that the card is forged, expired, revoked, or used without the consent of the issuer or cardholder.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Property Crimes / Financial Device Fraud)

entries.description: Computer Crimes Against Data or Programs—Intentionally accessing, modifying, destroying, copying, or taking possession of any computer, system, network, or data infrastructure without the consent of the owner and with intent to execute a fraud scheme or disrupt logistics.

Fraudulent Insurance Claims—Intentionally making or presenting any false or fraudulent statement or claim for payment of a loss under an insurance policy, or preparing records with intent to use them to support an unpermitted claim strategy.

Receiving Stolen Property—Intentionally receiving, buying, concealing, or facilitating the transfer of stolen movable property of another knowing or having reason to believe that the property was stolen.

Theft of Telecommunications or Public Utility Services—Intentionally obtaining or attempting to obtain telecommunications, electricity, gas, water, or corporate data infrastructure services without consent by use of a deceptive device, tampering manipulation, or fraudulent credentials.

Mail and Package Theft—Intentionally taking, concealing, or misdirecting mail matter or packages from a post office, common carrier truck, or private residential doorstep without the consent of the addressee or sender.

Attempted Embezzlement—Performing acts demonstrating unequivocally an intent to convert or misappropriate corporate, public, or bank assets held in a fiduciary capacity without consent, which are interrupted or fall short of actual asset transfer.

entries.subcategory: Bribery of a Public Servant / Official
entries.description: Attempted Public Bribery—Performing acts demonstrating unequivocally an intent to transfer, offer, or promise any property or advantage to a public servant to influence official action, which are caught, refused, or fall short of execution.

Arrest Record for Racketeering or Felony Embezzlement—The physical taking of a person into custody or formal booking on probable cause for state-level enterprise corruption (WOCCA), complex racketeering syndicates, or high-tier felony embezzlement, generating a permanent tracking entry prior to final court adjudication [CIB].

Arrest Record for Misdemeanor Fraud or Falsification—The physical taking of a person into custody or formal booking on probable cause for a misdemeanor-tier white-collar infraction, such as unsworn oral falsification or minor deceptive trade violations, logging a fingerprint trail prior to trial.

Warrant for Failure to Appear (Tax Fraud / White-Collar Nexus)—A judicially issued bench warrant commanding law enforcement to arrest an individual or corporate agent who failed to appear in court for an active criminal tax evasion, embezzlement, or corporate record falsification proceeding.

WOCCA Civil Restitution and Forfeiture Order—A non-criminal, post-investigation court injunction or final summary judgment ordering the complete dissolution, charter cancellation, or seizure of real property, corporate cash, and investments tied to a documented pattern of racketeering activity under Chapter 946.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Wisconsin Organized Crime Control Act - WOCCA)

entries.description: Simple Battery—Intentionally causing bodily harm to another human being by an act done without the express consent of the person harmed.
Substantiating Bodily Harm (Substantial Battery)—Intentionally causing substantial bodily harm to another human being by an act done without the express consent of the person harmed.

Aggravated Battery (Great Bodily Harm)—Intentionally causing great bodily harm to another human being by an act done without the express consent of the person harmed, under circumstances that create a substantial risk of death, permanent disfigurement, or prolonged impairment.

Domestic Abuse Battery—An act of battery under § 940.19 committed by an adult against his or her spouse or former spouse, against an adult with whom the person resides or formerly resided, or against an adult with whom the person has a child in common.

Battery to an Emergency Medical Care Provider—Intentionally causing bodily harm to an emergency medical services practitioner, an emergency medical responder, or a person acting under the direction of an emergency medical services practitioner, without the consent of the person harmed, while the victim is acting in an official capacity.

Strangulation and Suffocation—Intentionally impeding the normal breathing or circulation of blood by applying pressure on the throat or neck or by blocking the nose or mouth of another person without consent.

First-Degree Reckless Endangering Safety—Recklessly endangering another person's safety under circumstances which show utter disregard for human life. (Wisconsin's structural equivalent to aggravated felonious assault when a weapon is discharged or an extreme life-threatening environment is manufactured without completed battery).

Second-Degree Reckless Endangering Safety—Recklessly endangering another person's safety. (Wisconsin's structural baseline for severe menacing, vehicular close-calls, or non-contact weapons handling that places an individual in immediate peril without explicit proof of utter disregard for life).

Threats to Injure or Accuse of Crime (Coercion)—Maliciously threatening to accuse another of any crime or offense, or threatening injury to the person, property, or business of another, with intent to compel the person so threatened to do any act against his or her will or omit to do any lawful act.

Intimidation of a Witness / Victim (Aggravated Weapon Tier)—Knowingly and maliciously preventing or discouraging, or attempting to prevent or discourage, a victim or witness from reporting a crime, cooperating with law enforcement, or testifying, accompanied by the deployment, usage, or threat of a dangerous weapon.

Bomb Scares (Terroristic Threatening baseline)—Intentionally conveying or causing to be conveyed any false information, knowing such information to be false, concerning an attempt or alleged attempt being made or to be made to destroy any property by means of explosives or to cause bodily harm/death via chemical, biological, or nuclear device tracking matrices.

Hostage Taking / Kidnapping for Ransom—Seizing or confining any person without consent, and holding them as a hostage or for ransom to compel another person or governmental body to perform or abstain from any act, or as a shield or warning.

Stalking (Baseline)—Engaging in a course of conduct directed at a specific person that would cause a reasonable person to suffer serious emotional distress or to fear bodily injury or death to himself or herself or a member of his or her immediate family, and the actor's acts actually cause such fear or distress.

Aggravated Stalking—Engaging in a course of conduct directed at a specific person that causes reasonable fear of bodily injury or death, enhanced by the usage or threat of a dangerous weapon, targeting a minor, violating a protective injunction, or carrying a prior conviction within a 7-year tracking window.

Criminal Harassment—Engaging in a course of conduct with intent to harass or intimidate another person, including repeatedly striking, shoving, kicking, or otherwise subjecting the person to physical contact, or repeatedly engaging in acts that masquerade as communications or tracking without a legitimate purpose.

Hazing (Institutional/Student Networks)—Forcing or requiring abusive physical or mental conditioning as an element of initiation or admission into any student organization, fraternity, sorority, or institutional network, which creates a substantial risk of physical health degradation or bodily harm.

Attempted Aggravated Battery—Performing actions demonstrating unequivocally an intent to inflict great bodily harm to another without consent, which are caught, prevented, or fall short of completed physical execution.

Attempted Kidnapping—Performing acts demonstrating unequivocally an intent to seize, confine, or carry away another person by force or threat without consent, which are interrupted, caught, or fall short of containment execution.

entries.subcategory: Civil Domestic Abuse Restraining Orders / Injunctions
entries.description: Domestic Abuse Injunction / Restraining Order—A judicially issued civil protective mandate restricting an individual from entering the shared residence, place of employment, or immediate physical proximity of a petitioner who has established reasonable grounds to fear domestic abuse.

Intimidating a Witness (Baseline)—Except as provided in § 940.43, knowingly and maliciously preventing or discouraging, or attempting to prevent or discourage, any witness from attending or giving testimony at any trial, proceeding, or authorized inquiry.

Felony Intimidation of a Witness—Intentionally preventing or discouraging, or attempting to prevent or discourage, any person who has been or may be a witness from giving testimony, where the act is accompanied by force, threats of injury, or part of a conspiracy.

Simulating Legal Process—Sending or delivering to another any document which simulates a summons, complaint, writ, subpoena, or other judicial process with intent to lead the recipient to believe it is genuine.

Obstructing an Officer via Falsification—Knowingly providing false information to an officer with intent to mislead them in an official investigation, or fabricating evidence to implicate an innocent person.

Impersonating a Law Enforcement Officer—Intentionally impersonating a peace officer, warden, or state trooper with intent to mislead others into believing the actor is a law enforcement professional, or performing any official act under such simulation.

Arbitrary Public Records Refusal—An official record custodian or public servant willfully, arbitrarily, or capriciously refusing or delaying access to a legally formatted public records request under the Wisconsin Open Records Law framework.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Public Records and Property Regulations)

entries.description: Misconduct in Public Office—A public officer or public employee intentionally performing an act which they know is in excess of their lawful authority, intentionally failing to perform a mandatory duty required by law, or exercising authority with a known corrupt motive for personal advantage.

Private Interest in Public Contract—A public officer or employee acting in their official capacity to negotiate, bid, or enter into a public contract in which they carry a private pecuniary interest or stock allocation exceeding standard nominal statutory caps.
Communicating with Jurors—Attempting to influence the decision of any juror in a civil or criminal case by communicating with them outside of official court protocols, whether orally or in writing.

Intentional Disclosure of Protected Health Data—Knowingly and willfully violating state confidentiality perimeters by disclosing, stealing, or using patient healthcare records or criminal justice information abstracts for personal benefit or commercial leverage.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Public Records Privacy / Health Data Regulations)

entries.description: Impersonating a Public Officer—Falsely assuming or pretending to be a public officer, a public employee, a peace officer, or an inspector, and acting as such or compelling others to submit to faux regulatory authority.

Compounding a Crime—Accepting or altering any financial consideration, property asset, or reward upon an agreement or understanding that the actor will conceal a felony crime, withhold evidence, or abstain from criminal prosecution.

Falsification of Financial Statement—Intentionally making a false or fraudulent material representation on an officially submitted Statement of Financial Interest required of state public officials, candidates, or designated fiduciaries.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Ethics Code / Code of Official Conduct)

entries.description: Obstructing an Officer by False Sincerity—Knowingly delivering false information or placing an intentionally fabricated narrative line to a law enforcement officer with intent to mislead them in the performance of their official duties.

Unlawful Disclosure of CIB / TIME Data—An employee of a law enforcement agency intentionally accessing, utilizing, or disclosing confidential criminal history records, fingerprint files, or police database information for an unpermitted, non-law-enforcement purpose.

Punitive Contempt Adjudication—The intentional disobedience, resistance, or obstruction of the authority, process, or order of a court, prosecuted under criminal procedure rules to punish past non-compliance.
Resisting or Obstructing an Officer—Knowingly resisting or obstructing an officer while such officer is doing any act in an official capacity and with lawful authority.

Destroying or Altering Evidence (Hindering Apprehension)—Intentionally destroying, altering, hiding, or fabricating physical evidence, or placing false evidence, with intent to prevent the apprehension, prosecution, or conviction of a person who has committed a felony crime.

Harboring a Felon (Hindering Apprehension)—With intent to prevent the apprehension, prosecution, or conviction of a person who has committed a felony crime, knowingly harboring, lodging, or concealing that person.
Felony Bail Jumping—Intentionally violating any non-financial or financial condition of a bond or pre-trial release order executed during a pending felony charge or grand jury indictment.

Escape by a Convicted Felon—An individual sentenced or held in custody on a felony charge intentionally escaping or fleeing from a state prison, county jail, correctional facility, or the physical custody of a law enforcement officer.

Refusing to Aid Officer—Without a reasonable excuse, refusing or neglecting to render assistance to an officer when commanded to do so by an officer who has lawful authority to command such assistance in the execution of criminal arrest lines.
False Alarms—Intentionally giving or causing to be given a false alarm of fire or emergency medical need, or tampering with public safety communication lines or municipal signal boxes.

False Report of an Emergency—Intentionally conveying or causing to be conveyed to an emergency service provider false information concerning an emergency, or reporting a crime that did not happen to divert emergency personnel.

Fleeing or Eluding an Officer—Operating a motor vehicle on a highway and, after receiving a visual or audible signal from a traffic officer, knowingly fleeing or attempting to elude the officer by willful or wanton disregard of such signal or by increasing speed or extinguishing lights.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Vehicular Code / Crimes Against Safety)

entries.description: Treason Against the State / High Obstructive Subversion—Levying war against the state, adhering to its enemies, or giving them aid and comfort; or intentionally orchestrating the physical destruction of state government operation hubs to permanently seize administrative controls.

Misconduct in Public Office (Omission Tier)—A public officer or public employee intentionally failing or refusing to perform a mandatory, non-discretionary duty required of them by law, in their official capacity.

Misconduct in Office (Exercise of Unlawful Power)—A public officer or public employee intentionally performing an act which the officer knows is in excess of the officer's lawful authority, or which the officer knows is forbidden by law to be performed in that official capacity.

Misconduct in Office (Personal Benefit Tier)—A public officer or public employee intentionally falsifying profiles, overcharging fees, or exercising official authority to secure an unpermitted personal benefit, advantage, or financial gain for themselves or a third party.

Misconduct in Office (Financial Speculation Tier)—A public officer or public employee who, in an official capacity, buys or receives any claim, certificate, or public warrant at a discount, or speculates on state infrastructure assets with intent to secure personal financial gain from public liabilities.

Felony Lobbying Corrupt Acts—Any lobbyist, principal, or public officer who willfully falsifies a lobbying registry report, provides illegal financial consideration to a lawmaker to manipulate bill positioning, or falsifies required corporate expenditure statements under Chapter 13.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Lobbying Regulation and Governance Codes)

entries.description: Altering Legislative Documents—Intentionally destroying, defacing, secreting, or making a false entry in any official sessional record, enrolled bill, or legislative act filed or deposited with the state or assembly clerks.

Telecommunications Service Theft / Simulating Infrastructure—Manufacturing, distributing, or utilizing electronic device modifications, network spoofing programs, or intercepted utility signals to scan, clone, or siphon telecommunications bandwidth without merchant consent.

Filing a Fraudulent Lien—Intentionally directing, organizing, or filing a false lien, mock financing statement (UCC-1), or property cloud encumbrance against the real or personal property of a public officer, judge, or public employee knowing the filing is a fabrication and intended to harass or retaliate.

Unauthorized Use of an Entity's or Individual's Identity—Intentionally using or attempting to use a financial, personal, or administrative identifier of another individual or corporate entity, dead or alive, to avoid a civil or criminal process, or to hinder law enforcement tracking loops.

Filing Fraudulent Liens—Intentionally presenting for filing or recording any lien, financing statement, or cloud on property title against a public official or public employee knowing that the document is groundless or fabricated.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Property Crimes / Clouds on Title)

entries.description: Corporate Audit Record Deception—Intentionally concealing, altering, shredding, or destroying corporate records, bank ledgers, or trust documents while a state DFI or DOJ financial investigation is active or pending.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Uniform Securities Act / Corporate Audit Protection)

entries.description: Refusal to Exhibit Driver License—The intentional failure or refusal of a motor vehicle operator to display or permit an officer to physically inspect their operator's license or state identity document upon a lawful traffic safety stop.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Vehicular Code / Police Intake Protection)

entries.description: Simulating Court Operations—Organizing, convening, or participating in any unpermitted or mock judicial tribunal, common law court, or administrative panel intending to issue fake declarations or coercion orders against state citizens or property assets.

Unauthorized Disclosure of Justice Data—Accessing the state Transaction Information for Management of Enforcement (TIME) network or CIB databases to copy, leak, or disclose confidential criminal justice histories or tracking records for unpermitted or personal usage.

Obstructing an Officer via False Information—Knowingly giving false information, a fabricated identity, or false statements to an officer while that officer is acting in an official capacity with lawful authority, with intent to mislead the officer.

Unauthorized Practice of Law—Engaging in the practice of law, appearing in a court of record on behalf of another, or holding oneself out as an attorney authorized to practice law in Wisconsin without a valid active license from the State Bar.

Failure to Provide DNA Sample—An institutional prisoner, parolee, or felon on extended supervision intentionally refusing or failing to submit a biological sample (buccal swab or blood) requested by the department for mandatory integration into the state DOJ identification databank.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (DOJ Administration / DNA Bank Code)

entries.description: Simulating Emergency Safety Alerts—Interfering with, replicating, or transmitting a fraudulent civil defense alert, weather sirens network system signal, or municipal emergency evacuation broadcast with intent to trigger mass public panic or infrastructure gridlock.

Impersonating a Transit or Utility Worker—Falsely representing oneself to be an employee or authorized structural inspector of a public utility, telecommunications carrier, or cooperative association, with intent to secure unpermitted entry into private residences, grid networks, or secure facilities.

Injury to Transmission Lines / Sabotage—Intentionally cutting, damaging, disrupting, or misaligning any public utility line, fiber optic array, electrical substation component, or emergency communication tower infrastructure without consent.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Property Crimes / Interruption of Utilities)

entries.description: False Emergency Reports—Intentionally dialing 911, activating an emergency duress node, or communicating an unverified emergency report to a public protection dispatch agency knowing that no emergency exists.

Harassment (Municipal Adoption)—Engaging in a course of conduct or repeatedly committing acts that intimidate or alarm another person, including non-physical digital messaging or tracking within city lines, which serve no legitimate purpose.
entries.statuteTitle: Milwaukee Code of Ordinances (Public Order Regulations)

entries.description: Unlawful Municipal Water Connection—Making an unpermitted connection or tapping into any public city water main or sewer lateral without authorization from the commissioner of public works.
entries.statuteTitle: Milwaukee Code of Ordinances (Water and Sewer Systems)

entries.description: Attempted Animal Fighting—Performing acts demonstrating unequivocally an intent to instigate, finance, or operate a dogfighting or animal-fighting exhibition, which are interrupted or prevented prior to execution.

Active Arrest Warrant (Felony Animal Cruelty Nexus)—A judicially authorized order commanding law enforcement to arrest a named individual based on a showing of probable cause for felony-tier animal torture, mutilation, or abandonment.

Active DNR Conservation Warrant—A judicially authorized arrest warrant commanding natural resource wardens or law enforcement to apprehend an individual for uncorrected big game poaching out of season or egregious environmental resource theft.

Arrest Booking for Animal Fighting Operations—The physical taking of a person into custody or formal booking on probable cause for felony-tier animal fighting, baiting enterprise, or dogfighting rings, generating a permanent tracking entry prior to final court adjudication.

Arrest Booking for Telecommunication Interception—The physical taking of a person into custody or formal booking on probable cause for felony electronic wiretapping, surveillance interception, or data-line bugging, generating a permanent tracking footprint prior to trial.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Criminal Procedure Code / Electronic Surveillance)

entries.description: Warrant for Failure to Appear (Weights & Measures Nexus)—A judicially issued bench warrant commanding law enforcement to arrest an individual or corporate trader who failed to appear in court for an active criminal metric scaling fraud, device tampering, or agricultural consumer protection proceeding.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Criminal Procedure Code / Trade Enforcement)

entries.description: Warrant for Failure to Appear (DNR Conservation Nexus)—A judicially issued bench warrant commanding law enforcement or DNR wardens to apprehend an individual who failed to appear in court for an active criminal poaching, commercial wildlife trafficking, or biological resource violation.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Criminal Procedure Code / Conservation Code)

entries.subcategory: Bribery of a Public Servant / Official
entries.description: Unlawful Lobbying Gifts—Any lobbyist or principal intentionally giving, offering, or promising anything of pecuniary value (including campaign contributions outside allowed window loops or illegal hospitality gifts) to an elected state official, legislative employee, or candidate for state office.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Legislative Lobbying Code / Ethics Regulations)

entries.description: Act of Terrorism—Intentionally committing a felony act causing mass bodily harm, death, or catastrophic infrastructure damage, with the explicit intent to intimidate or coerce a civilian population or influence the policy of a government unit by intimidation or coercion.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Crimes Against Life and Bodily Security)

entries.description: Fraudulent Academic Credentials—Operating an unaccredited post-secondary institution and falsely representing it as accredited, or conferring fake academic degrees, diplomas, or credentials for financial compensation.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Higher Education Trade and Fraud Code)

entries.description: Habitual Truancy (Parental Failure)—A parent or guardian failing to compel a child's school attendance after receiving formal statutory notice that the child qualifies as a habitual truant (absent from school without an acceptable excuse for part or all of 5 or more days on which fame is held during a school semester).
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Public Instruction Code / Compulsory Attendance)

entries.description: Operating Aircraft Under Influence—Operating an aircraft in the air, on the ground, or on the water while under the influence of an intoxicant or a restricted controlled substance, or in a careless or reckless manner so as to endanger the life or property of another.

Operating While Intoxicated (1st Offense)—Operating a motor vehicle on a public highway while under the influence of an intoxicant, a controlled substance, or a combination thereof, to a degree which renders the person incapable of safely driving; or driving with a blood alcohol concentration (BAC) of 0.08% or more.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Vehicular Code / Rules of the Road)

entries.description: Operating While Intoxicated (2nd Offense)—Operating a motor vehicle on a public highway within 10 years of a prior OWI conviction, suspension, or implied consent refusal.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Vehicular Code / Rules of the Road)

entries.description: Commercial Driver License OWI—Driving or operating a commercial motor vehicle (CMV) on a public highway with a blood alcohol concentration (BAC) of 0.04% or more, or while under the influence of an intoxicant.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Vehicular Code / Commercial Driver Standards)

entries.description: Inattentive Driving / Handheld Device Use—Operating a motor vehicle on a public highway while using a handheld wireless telephone or text-messaging device, or engaging in any conduct that interferes with the safe operation of the vehicle.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Vehicular Code / Rules of the Road)

entries.description: Texting in School/Construction Zone—Operating a motor vehicle on a public highway and using a handheld wireless device to compose, send, or read an electronic message while driving through a posted school zone, school crossing, or active highway maintenance construction area.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Vehicular Code / Rules of the Road)
entries.statuteNumber: Wis. Stat. § 346.89(4m) [School Zone / Construction Zone Track]

entries.description: Reckless Driving—Endangering the safety of any person or property by the negligent operation of a vehicle on a public highway or on any premises open to the public for vehicular traffic.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Vehicular Code / Rules of the Road)

entries.description: Speed Unreasonable and Imprudent (Careless Driving)—Operating a motor vehicle on a public highway at a speed greater than is reasonable and prudent under the conditions and having regard for the actual and potential hazards then existing.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Vehicular Code / Rules of the Road)

entries.description: Overtaking or Passing Stopped School Bus—The operator of a vehicle overtaking or meeting from the front or rear any school bus which has stopped on a highway and is displaying flashing red warning lights, failing to stop the vehicle not less than 20 feet from the bus and remain stopped until the bus resumes motion or the flashing lights are extinguished.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Vehicular Code / Rules of the Road)

entries.description: Speeding (General Moving Infraction)—Operating a motor vehicle on a public highway in excess of a statutorily established or posted speed limit.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Vehicular Code / Rules of the Road)

entries.subcategory: Hit and Run — Property Damage Only (Misdemeanor)
entries.description: Hit and Run (Attended Vehicle Property Damage)—Failing to immediately stop a vehicle at the scene of an accident involving damage to an attended vehicle, or failing to remain at the scene to provide identity, operator license, and registration details to the other driver.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Vehicular Code / Accidents and Accident Reports)

entries.subcategory: Hit and Run — Property Damage Only (Misdemeanor)
entries.description: Felony Hit and Run—The operator of a vehicle involved in an accident resulting in injury or death to any person, failing to immediately stop, remain at the scene to provide identifying data, and render reasonable assistance to the injured person.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Vehicular Code / Accidents and Accident Reports)

entries.description: Operating After Suspension (OWS)—Operating a motor vehicle on a public highway during any period in which the person's operating privilege is suspended by the state or an administrative order.

Operating Unregistered Vehicle—Operating or permitting the operation of a motor vehicle on a public highway that is not registered with WisDOT or for which the registration has been suspended, revoked, or expired.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Vehicular Code / Vehicle Title and Registration)

entries.description: Commercial Driver License Disqualification—An administrative declaration by WisDOT forcing the long-term or permanent removal of an individual's commercial motor vehicle (CMV) operating authority following the accumulation of major traffic offenses, railroad crossing violations, or serious safety failures under FMCSR guidelines.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Vehicular Code / Commercial Driver Standards)

entries.description: Operating While Revoked (OWI Nexus)—Operating a motor vehicle on a public highway during a period in which the person's operating privilege is revoked by WisDOT or a criminal court judge following a prior major conviction (such as an OWI, felony fleeing, or fatal hit and run).

entries.subcategory: Hit and Run — Property Damage Only (Misdemeanor)
entries.description: Hit and Run (Property Damage Only)—Failing to immediately stop a vehicle after an accident on a highway or public premises involving damage to an attended vehicle, or failing to remain at the scene to fulfill statutory identity and insurance data exchange requirements.

entries.subcategory: Hit and Run — Property Damage Only (Misdemeanor)
entries.description: Hit and Run Involving Injury or Death—Failing to immediately stop a vehicle after an accident on a highway or public premises resulting in injury or death to any person, or failing to remain at the scene to provide mandatory reasonable assistance and statutory identity data exchange.

Vehicle Registration Suspension—An administrative order issued by WisDOT suspending a vehicle's registration or title plates following an owner's failure to maintain mandatory liability insurance, non-payment of traffic fines, or refusal to comply with emissions testing.

entries.subcategory: Hit and Run — Property Damage Only (Misdemeanor)
entries.description: Hit and Run (Aggravated Death/Injury Tier)—An operator of a vehicle involved in an accident resulting in great bodily harm or death to any person failing to immediately stop their vehicle at the scene, fulfill mandatory identification exchanges, and render reasonable medical assistance.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Vehicular Code / Accidents and Accident Reports)
altCitations: Leaving the Scene with Great Bodily Harm or Death

entries.description: Operating While Revoked (Criminal Tier)—Operating a motor vehicle on a public highway knowing that one's operating privilege has been suspended or revoked, where the underlying revocation was caused by an OWI, chemical test refusal, or vehicular homicide tracking index.

Operating Vehicle with Suspended/Altered Registration—Driving or permitting the operation of a motor vehicle on a public highway when the vehicle's state registration is suspended, revoked, or canceled, or displaying a simulated, altered, or unassigned license plate.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Vehicular Code / Vehicle Title and Registration)

entries.description: First-Degree Sexual Assault—Intentionally engaging in sexual intercourse or sexual contact with another person without consent, where the act causes great bodily harm, or is executed by use or threat of a dangerous weapon, or occurs with a non-consenting person aided by group force/co-actors.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Crimes Against Life and Bodily Security)

entries.description: Second-Degree Sexual Assault—Intentionally engaging in sexual intercourse or sexual contact with another person without consent by use or threat of force or violence, or with a person the actor knows is mentally incapacitated, physically helpless, or unconscious.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Crimes Against Life and Bodily Security)

entries.description: Third-Degree Sexual Assault—Intentionally engaging in sexual intercourse with another person without that person's consent, absent aggravating weapon, injury, or force factors required under first or second degree frameworks.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Crimes Against Life and Bodily Security)

entries.description: Fourth-Degree Sexual Assault—Intentionally engaging in sexual contact with another person without that person's consent (covers unpermitted physical touching of intimate areas for sexual gratification).
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Crimes Against Life and Bodily Security)

entries.description: First-Degree Sexual Assault of a Child—Intentionally engaging in sexual intercourse or sexual contact with a child who has not attained the age of 12 years.

Second-Degree Sexual Assault of a Child—Intentionally engaging in sexual intercourse or sexual contact with another person who has attained the age of 12 years but has not attained the age of 16 years.

Repeated Sexual Assault of Same Child—Engaging in 3 or more violations of child sexual assault under § 948.02 against the same child within a specified tracking window, combined into a single continuous count index.

Possession of Child Pornography—Knowingly possessing, viewing, reproducing, downloading, or storing any photograph, film, videotape, print, or digital image depicting a child engaging in sexually explicit conduct.

Production of Explicit Child Material—Knowingly employing, using, persuading, enticing, or coercing a child under 18 years of age to engage in sexually explicit conduct for the purpose of photographing, filming, or recording.

Computer Enticement of a Child—Using a computer system, mobile telephone network, or digital electronic communication service to communicate with a child under 18 years of age with intent to induce or entice the child to engage in illegal sexual activity.

Public Sexual Indecency—Intentionally engaging in an open act of sexual intercourse or sexual contact in a public place under circumstances in which the actor knows their conduct will be openly observed; or indecently exposing a sex organ in a public setting.

Sexual Exposure to a Minor—Intentionally exposing one's genitals, pubic area, or anus to a child under the age of 16 years, or causing a child under 16 to expose their own intimate areas, for purposes of sexual gratification or arousal of the actor.

Professional Sexual Exploitation—Any person who is or represents themselves to be a therapist, physician, or professional counselor intentionally having sexual contact or sexual intercourse with a patient or client during, or under the pretext of, ongoing professional treatment or consultation.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Crimes Against Life and Bodily Security)

entries.description: Capture of Intimate Representation—Intentionally capturing an intimate representation of another person (via hidden cameras, smartphone recording tools, or electronic bugs) without the person's consent, under circumstances where the person has a reasonable expectation of privacy.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Crimes Against Reputation and Privacy)

entries.description: Soliciting a Child for Prostitution—Intentionally soliciting, encouraging, or enticing any child under the age of 18 years to practice prostitution, or receiving anything of value from the commercial sexual exploitation of a child.

Exposing Child to Harmful Matter—Intentionally exhibiting, selling, distributing, or displaying any harmful explicit material (photographs, printed media, or digital representations showing nudity or explicit conduct) to a child under the age of 18 years.

Institutional Custodial Sexual Assault—Any correctional employee, state agent, or facility contractor intentionally engaging in sexual intercourse or sexual contact with an inmate or resident confined in a staff-monitored prison, jail, workhouse, or secure care center.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Crimes Against Life and Bodily Security)

entries.description: Failure to Maintain Sex Offender Registration—Knowingly failing to comply with mandatory sex offender registration updates, failing to verify a changing residential address, employment location, or school enrollment ledger within required statutory windows.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Department of Corrections / Registry Codes)

entries.description: Fourth-Degree Sexual Assault of a Child—Intentionally engaging in sexual contact (intentional touching of intimate parts or clothing covering them for sexual gratification) with a child under the age of 16 years, without consent, absent the use of force, threat, or weapons.

Soliciting a Child for Prostitution—Intentionally soliciting, encouraging, or enticing any child under the age of 18 years to practice prostitution, or slanting a transactional payment asset to secure a commercial sex act with a minor.

Exposing Minor to Harmful Material—Intentionally exhibiting, showing, or displaying harmful or explicit obscene material (including digital prints, photographs, or simulations depicting explicit nudity or ultimate sexual conduct) to a child under the age of 18 years, or selling/loaning such material to a child for commercial profit.

Arrest Booking for Felony Sexual Assault—The physical taking into custody, fingerprinting, and photographic logging of an individual based on probable cause for felony sexual assault under § 940.225, creating an un-adjudicated tracking footprint.

Arrest Booking for Child Sex Trafficking—The physical arrest and booking of an individual based on probable cause for trafficking minors for commercial sexual servitude under § 948.051, creating a pre-trial tracking footprint.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Criminal Procedure / Child Exploitation Protocols)

entries.subcategory: Active Sex Offender Registry Fugitive Bench Warrants
entries.description: Warrant for Failure to Appear (Registry Nexus)—A judicially issued bench warrant commanding law enforcement to arrest a registered sex offender who failed to appear in court for an active felony registry non-compliance, address update failure, or evasion proceeding under § 301.45.

Unauthorized Recording of Intimate Areas—Capturing an intimate representation of another person without their consent, in a place where the person has a reasonable expectation of privacy, or distributing such a representation knowing it was captured without consent.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Crimes Against Reputation and Civil Liberties)

entries.description: Arrest Booking for Felony Sexual Assault (Alternate Citation)—The physical taking of a person into custody or formal booking on probable cause for felony sexual assault or forcible rape, generating a permanent tracking fingerprint trail and immediate state Crime Information Bureau (CIB) database entries prior to trial.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Criminal Procedure Code / Sex Offense Intake)

entries.description: Arrest Booking for Human Trafficking—The physical booking and taking into custody on probable cause for felony sex trafficking, human trafficking, or commercial sexual servitude, establishing immediate tracking loops prior to formal trial.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Criminal Procedure Code / Human Trafficking Intake)

entries.description: Warrant for Failure to Appear (DNA Databank Nexus)—A judicially issued bench warrant commanding law enforcement to arrest an individual who failed to appear in court or report to a testing facility for a mandatory pre-trial DNA specimen collection order issued under state felony sex offense protocols.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Criminal Procedure Code / DNA Databank)

entries.description: Attempted Child Pornography Production—Performing acts demonstrating unequivocally an intent to employ, use, persuade, entice, or coerce a child under 18 years of age to engage in sexually explicit conduct for the purpose of photographing, filming, or recording, which are prevented or cut off prior to successful capture.

Public Lewdness or Window Peeping (Municipal)—Loitering or crouching in a public or private space for the purpose of looking, peeping, or peering into the windows, doors, or private residential openings of another without consent, or engaging in minor lewd gestures below state criminal code thresholds.
entries.statuteTitle: Milwaukee Code of Ordinances (Public Safety Regulations)

entries.description: Unlawful Hazardous Waste Disposal—Intentionally transporting, storing, treating, or disposing of any hazardous waste compound without a valid DNR license, or making false statements in mandatory toxic manifest ledgers.

Commercial Food Adulteration—Manufacturing, packaging, holding, or offering for sale any adulterated, contaminated, or filthy food product intended for human consumption within state commerce pipelines.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Wisconsin Food Code / Pure Food Law)

entries.description: Public Assistance Provider Fraud—Knowingly making false statements or concealing material facts to claim unpermitted payments under a medical assistance program, or offering/accepting illegal kickbacks or rebates to alter referral tracks.

Active Environmental Felony Warrant—An open, judicially executed arrest warrant commanding law enforcement officers and DNR wardens to locate and physically apprehend an individual or corporate agent indicted for high-tier toxic dumping, hazardous waste storage violations, or environmental record falsification.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Criminal Procedure Code / DNR Enforcement)

entries.description: Attempted Healthcare Fraud—Performing acts demonstrating unequivocally an intent to submit fraudulent billing ledgers or execute an illegal kickback scheme under a medical assistance program, which are detected, intercepted, or fall short of completed transaction draw.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Public Assistance Fraud Code / Inchoate)

entries.description: Fraudulent Wage Non-Payment—An employer intentionally, and with intent to defraud, failing, refusing, or omitting to pay a worker all accrued wages or earned compensation due, where the unpaid asset balance crosses felony limits.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (DWD Employment Regulations / Wage Payments)

entries.description: Unlawful Employment of Minors—An employer or parent permitting, hiring, or forcing a child under 18 years of age to work in a hazardous, prohibited, or sexually explicit commercial trade, or violating statutory hour limits post-warning.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (DWD Employment Regulations / Child Labor)

entries.subcategory: Professional Licensing Discipline & Institutional Welfare Actions
entries.description: CPA License Revocation—A formal administrative disciplinary order issued by the Accounting Examining Board permanently revoking a Certified Public Accountant's registration following a conviction for a financial felony, gross negligence in asset audits, or intentional balance sheet fraud.

Active Felony Wage Fraud Warrant—An open, judicially executed arrest warrant commanding law enforcement officers to locate and physically apprehend an employer, company executive, or corporate officer indicted for high-tier felony wage theft or fraudulent non-payment of earnings.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Criminal Procedure Code / Labor Violations)

entries.description: Financial Exploitation of a Vulnerable Adult—An individual in a position of trust, confidence, or fiduciary authority intentionally taking, stealing, obtaining, or using the property, cash, or financial assets of an elder or vulnerable adult with intent to defraud and deprive them of their estate value.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Crimes Against Children and Vulnerable Adults Code)

entries.description: Criminal Tax Evasion—Willfully attempting in any manner to evade or defeat any tax imposed under Chapter 71, or filing completely fabricated or fraudulent income, corporate, or franchise tax returns with intent to defraud the state.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (General Taxation / Income and Franchise Taxes)

entries.description: Active Fiduciary Fraud Warrant—An open, judicially executed arrest warrant commanding law enforcement officers to locate and physically apprehend an individual, fiduciary agent, or legal guardian indicted for high-tier felony financial exploitation of an elder or vulnerable adult.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Criminal Procedure Code / Elder Exploitation)

entries.description: Attempted Tax Evasion—Performing acts demonstrating unequivocally an intent to evade or defeat state income or corporate franchise taxes by creating fraudulent transactional ledgers, which are intercepted, detected, or audited prior to final tax submission draw.

Antitrust Restraint of Trade—Intentionally engaging in a conspiracy, trust, or combination in restraint of trade or commerce, including horizontal price-fixing, market allocation, or collaborative bid-rigging schemes on public or private procurement contracts.

entries.subcategory: Bribery of a Public Servant / Official
entries.description: Public Contract Kickback—An independent contractor, vendor, or public agent offering, giving, soliciting, or receiving any unpermitted rebate, percentage compensation, or kickback return as an inducement to influence or steer the award of a public works or municipal procurement contract.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Crimes Against Administration / Bribery and Official Misconduct)

entries.description: Public Vendor Procurement Debarment—An administrative declaration of non-responsibility or formal exclusion issued by the Department of Administration (DOA) debarring a contractor or company from bidding on public contracts following a state antitrust conviction, systemic wage theft history, or matching federal SAM exclusion listings.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (State Procurement and Vendor Compliance Code)

entries.description: Attempted Restraint of Trade—Performing acts demonstrating unequivocally an intent to establish an anti-competitive price-fixing or bid-rigging ring, which are detected, reported by corporate compliance audits, or intercepted by state investigators prior to the final competitive public bid opening.

Boating Under the Influence (1st Offense)—Operating a motorboat or manipulating water skis, aquaplanes, or similar devices on the navigable waters of the state while under the influence of an intoxicant, or with a prohibited alcohol concentration of 0.08% or more.

Criminal BUI (2nd Offense)—Operating a motorboat or manipulating water skis, aquaplanes, or similar devices on navigable waters within 5 years of a prior OWI, BUI, or chemical test refusal conviction.

Maritime Implied Consent Refusal—An operator of a motorboat intentionally refusing to submit to a lawful chemical test of their breath, blood, or urine requested by a law enforcement officer or DNR warden who has probable cause to arrest the person for boating under the influence.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Navigable Waters / Boating Safety Administration)

entries.description: Attempted Criminal BUI—Performing acts demonstrating unequivocally an intent to start, navigate, or operate a motorboat on navigable waters while under the influence of an intoxicant, where the attempt executes at a repeat offender level (2nd or subsequent track) and is intercepted or halted by law enforcement or DNR wardens prior to successful watercraft locomotion.

Bribery of Athletic Participant—Intentionally offering, promising, or giving anything of value to any participant, official, coach, or manager in any athletic contest, match, or sporting event with intent to influence them to lose, limit the margin of victory, or cheat to manipulate sports wagering outcomes.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Crimes Against Public Policy / Gambling Code)

entries.description: Attempted Commercial Gambling—Performing acts demonstrating unequivocally an intent to open, operate, or finance an illegal bookmaking or commercial card enterprise, which are halted by law enforcement or regulatory sweeps prior to the execution of active betting operations or card pool mechanics.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Crimes Against Public Policy / Inchoate)

entries.description: Petty Gambling (Municipal)—Participating in an unlicensed raffle, local poker pool, or street dice game, or possessing nominal unpermitted video gaming mechanisms within municipal limits below state commercial tracking ceilings.
entries.statuteTitle: Milwaukee Code of Ordinances (Public Safety Regulations)

entries.description: Substantiated Child Abuse/Neglect—A final administrative determination by a county Child Protective Services (CPS) agency or DCF panel confirming, by a preponderance of the evidence, that an individual committed physical abuse, sexual abuse, emotional damage, or severe neglect against a child.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Children's Code / Child Protection Registries)

entries.description: Substantiated Caregiver Misconduct—A final administrative determination by a DHS panel confirming, by a preponderance of the evidence, that an individual employed in a state-regulated entity committed abuse, financial exploitation, neglect, or misappropriation of property against a vulnerable adult or nursing home resident.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Health and Safety Codes / Caregiver Misconduct Registries)

entries.description: Capias / Warrant for Failure to Appear (CPS Nexus)—A judicially issued bench warrant or capias commanding law enforcement to physically apprehend a parent, guardian, or respondent who failed to appear in court for an active child abuse fact-finding or Child in Need of Protection or Services (CHIPS) proceeding.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Children's Code / Court Enforcement Procedure)

entries.description: Criminal Damage to Property (Felony Tier)—Intentionally causing physical damage to the non-residential, commercial, or public property of another without consent, where the reduction in value or cost of repair exceeds the felony statutory threshold.

Unlawful Tenant Holdover—A tenant failing to pay rent, violating material lease terms, or refusing to vacate a rental property after receiving a formal statutory 5-day, 14-day, or 28-day written notice to quit or remedy, causing an eviction action to be filed and adjudicated.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Property Code / Landlord and Tenant Relations)

entries.description: Active Writ of Restitution—A judicially issued execution order commanding the county sheriff to locate, physically remove an unlawful holdover occupant from a designated parcel, and seize or secure remaining property assets to satisfy outstanding landlord judgments.
entries.statuteTitle: Wisconsin Statutes (Small Claims Procedure / Execution of Eviction)

entries.subcategory: Bribery of a Public Servant / Official
entries.description: Bribery of a Public Servant
Obstructing Government Operations
Tampering with Physical Evidence
Failure to Appear – Willful failure to appear before a court as required by a lawful order or citation; penal code track; classified based on severity of underlying offense.
Bail Jumping – Failing to appear as required after being released on bail or recognizance; criminal procedure enforcement track; separate charge that may run concurrently with underlying offense.
Escape in the First Degree
Escape in the Second Degree
Furnishing Contraband to Inmate
Possession of Firearm by Felon
Carrying a Weapon Without Permit
Possession of Controlled Substance
Delivery / Manufacture of Methamphetamine or Cocaine
Drug Trafficking
Disorderly Conduct
Harassment
Violation of Order of Protection – Knowingly violating a valid court-issued civil or criminal order of protection.
Domestic Abuse Definitions – Pure definition section; contains no standalone criminal or civil offenses.
Child Maltreatment Findings – Definition framework for administrative/civil adjudication of abuse.
Child Care Facility Registry Violations – Operating or working in a child care facility with a disqualifying registry entry.
Professional Engineers – Fraud, deceit, gross negligence, or misconduct in engineering practice.
Contractor Violations – Working as a commercial contractor without a valid state license.
Contractor Civil Penalties – Committing residential contracting violations or failing to maintain a bond.
Nursing License Discipline – Fraud in procuring license, drug diversion, gross incompetence, or unfitness.
Medical Board Actions – Unprofessional conduct, gross negligence, or unauthorized prescription practices.
Pharmacy License Discipline – Professional misconduct, substitution of drugs, or violating drug safety laws.
Child Care Civil Penalties – Violating statutory child care standards or safety directives.
Abuse Reporting – Willful failure of a mandated reporter to report suspected child/adult abuse.
Food Establishment Violations – Manufacturing, selling, or holding adulterated or misbranded food.
Retail Food Permit – Operating a food facility without or in violation of an active health permit.
Waste Hauler Violations – Illegal dumping or transporting solid waste without a regional board authority license.
Environmental Civil Penalties – General administrative violations of pollution controls or board orders.
Hazardous Waste Violations – Unlawful storage, transport, treatment, or disposal of regulated hazardous waste.
Water Pollution Enforcement – Discharging unpermitted pollutants into waters of the state.
entries.statuteTitle: Water and Air Pollution Control Act

entries.description: Constitutional Disqualifier – Absolute constitutional bar for those convicted of infamous crimes/felonies from holding public office.

entries.subcategory: Bribery of a Public Servant / Official
entries.description: Bribery/Public Corruption – Offering, giving, or receiving unlawful benefit to alter official action.
Election Disqualification – Committing voter fraud, campaign finance fraud, or illegal voting acts.
Public Office Ineligibility Findings – Judicial or legislative finding of malfeasance or constitutional default.
Driving While Suspended – Operating a motor vehicle while state driving privileges are actively suspended.
Failure to Appear (Traffic) – Failing to respond to a signed traffic citation or court summons.
CDL Disqualification – Violations triggering mandatory CDL suspension (e.g., DUI in commercial vehicle).
Vehicle Registration Suspension – Operating a vehicle with a registration suspended for insurance lapses.
Child Abuse Registry Findings – True administrative finding of child abuse/neglect by preponderance of evidence.
Adult Maltreatment Registry Findings – True administrative finding of abuse/exploitation of an endangered adult.
Professional Misconduct Findings – Formal determination of rule or ethics violation by a state licensing board.
Healthcare Exclusion Registry – Adjudication triggering inclusion on federal OIG exclusion list.
Medicaid Exclusion Actions – Program fraud, billing abuse, or kickbacks in Medicaid delivery.
Licensing Board Final Orders – Issuance of final disciplinary orders following a board hearing.
Attorney Discipline – Violations of the Rules of Professional Conduct, trust account fraud, or felony convictions.
Real Estate Licensing – Practicing without a license, misrepresentation, or mishandling earnest money.
Insurance Producer Discipline – Fraudulent insurance practices, premium diversion, or material misrepresentation.
CPA Discipline – Fraud in obtaining certificate, gross negligence, or dishonesty in financial audits.
Educator Licensure Discipline – Felony convictions, child maltreatment findings, test security violations, or ethical breaches.
Federal OIG LEIE – Mandatory or permissive exclusion due to Medicare/Medicaid fraud, patient abuse, or health-related felonies.
NPDB Reporting – Malpractice payments, clinical privilege restrictions, or professional society actions.
Arkansas Medicaid Exclusion – Fraud, programmatic overbilling, or provider integrity defaults in state programs.
State Police Employment Disqualifiers – Felony convictions, domestic violence convictions, or patterns of misconduct.
Childcare Employment Disqualifiers – True maltreatment findings, violent crimes, or sexual offenses barring childcare employment.
Long-Term Care Worker Exclusions – Convictions for abuse, neglect, exploitation, or theft from vulnerable adults.
School Employee Background Disqualifiers – Disqualifying offenses including sexual misconduct, violence, or drug distribution.
Child Maltreatment Central Registry – True administrative finding of child abuse/neglect.
Adult Maltreatment Central Registry – True finding of abuse, neglect, or exploitation of vulnerable adults.
Sex Offender Registry – Criminal conviction for a qualifying sexual or kidnapping offense.
OIG Exclusion List – Federal healthcare program exclusions or health fraud convictions.
NURSYS – State nursing board final disciplinary orders/actions reported to national database.
NPDB – Professional malpractice payouts and disciplinary actions reported to national practitioner roster.
AR Contractor Registry – Formal licensing board citations and code violations logged in state compliance system.
Production of Child Pornography – Employing, using, or persuading a minor to engage in sexually explicit conduct for visual recording.
Possession of Child Pornography – Knowingly possessing, viewing, or downloading visual depictions of a minor engaging in sexually explicit conduct.
Sexual Grooming of a Minor – Using electronic or physical communication to induce a minor to engage in illicit sexual activity.
Trafficking of Persons (Sexual) – Recruiting, harboring, or transporting a person for the purpose of commercial sex through force, fraud, or coercion.
Nonconsensual Sexual Recording (Revenge Porn) – Knowingly distributing intimate images of another person without consent to cause harm or distress.
EMS Discipline – Gross negligence, practicing beyond scope, or drug diversion by an EMT/Paramedic.
Psychology License Discipline – Professional misconduct, dual relationships, fraud, or practicing without a license. Regulated by the Arkansas Psychology Board.

Licensed Professional Counselor / MFT License Discipline – Professional misconduct, dual relationships, fraud, or practicing without a license. Regulated by the Arkansas Board of Examiners in Counseling (LPCs, Marriage & Family Therapists).
Long-Term Care Facility Enforcement – Facility failure to maintain safety standards resulting in resident injury or neglect.
Substance Abuse Treatment Licensing – Operating an uncertified rehabilitation center or violating state methadone dispensing rules.
Underground Storage Tanks (UST) – Operating an unregistered tank, failing to report leaks, or bypassing fuel delivery red tags.
Air Permitting Specifics – Constructing or operating a stationary air emission source without or in violation of a Title V permit.
entries.statuteTitle: Water and Air Pollution Control Act

entries.description: Asbestos Licensing – Performing asbestos abatement or demolition without a license, supervisor certificate, or proper wet-method disposal.

Environmental Remediation – Voluntary Clean-Up Program (VCP/Brownfields) – Intentionally falsifying site assessments or remedial reports within the state voluntary cleanup track; DEQ Brownfields program.
entries.statuteTitle: Voluntary Clean-Up / Petroleum Storage Tank Act

entries.description: Environmental Remediation – Petroleum Storage Tank (PST) Program – Falsifying or failing to complete required remediation reports under the state Petroleum Storage Tank cleanup authority; separate DEQ regulatory track from VCP.
Federal SAM Exclusions – Broad administrative inclusion based on systemic fraud, default, or bad-faith contract performance.
Federal Debarment (FAR 9.4) – Formal administrative determination that a contractor is non-responsible due to a criminal conviction or serious civil breach.
Federal Suspension (FAR 9.4) – Temporary exclusion of a contractor pending the completion of an investigation or legal proceeding based on indictments or adequate evidence.
FINRA Bars – Failure to comply with requests for information (Rule 8210), insider trading, conversion of funds, or market manipulation.
SEC Administrative Orders – Serious violations of federal securities law, fraudulent disclosure schemes, or underlying financial felonies.
Investment Adviser Discipline – Breaching fiduciary duties, misleading advisory clients, or running hidden fee schemes.
NMLS Enforcement Actions – Failing to disclose material criminal histories, escrow account skimming, or deceptive loan packaging.
entries.statuteTitle: Secure and Fair Enforcement for Mortgage Licensing Act

entries.description: Mortgage Loan Originator (MLO) Revocations – Statutory disqualification resulting from a financial felony or a pattern of regulatory consumer fraud.
GSA SAM Exclusions – General non-procurement or procurement defaults; systemic structural compliance failures.
entries.statuteTitle: Federal Property and Administrative Services Act

entries.description: HUD Debarments – Violating the Fair Housing Act, equity diversion, or construction kickbacks on HUD-backed projects.
USDA Exclusions – Falsifying agricultural loan logs, food safety defaults, or fraud in federal nutrition assistance programs.
Tribal Court Civil/Criminal Orders – Domestic violence protection order violations or civil tort adjudications inside tribal boundaries.
entries.statuteTitle: Indian Tribal Justice Act / Individual Tribal Codes

entries.description: UCMJ Convictions – Court-martial convictions for violent acts, theft, espionage, drug distribution, or conduct unbecoming.
Computer Fraud – Accessing a computer, system, or network to devise a scheme to defraud or obtain money/property.
Identity Theft – Knowingly using or attempting to use personal identifying information of another person without consent for financial gain.
Unregistered Securities Sales – Offering or selling any investment security in Arkansas unless registered, exempt, or a federal covered security.
Miscellaneous Election Fraud – Tampering with voting machines, ballot box stuffing, or falsifying voter registration materials.
Unlawful Use of Official Position – A public official using their office to secure special privileges or exemptions for personal financial gain.
entries.statuteTitle: Code of Ethics for Public Officers

entries.description: Failure to File Statement of Financial Interest – Willful failure of a candidate or designated public official to file required asset/income disclosure forms.
Juvenile Transfer to Adult Court – Statutory mechanism allowing or forcing a juvenile (14 or older) to be tried as an adult for capital, violent, or Class Y/A/B felonies.
Confidentiality & Record Sealing – Unlawful disclosure of confidential juvenile court records, or rules governing the automatic sealing of delinquency files.
Probation – Court-ordered supervision in lieu of or following incarceration; conditions may include reporting, drug testing, curfew, and community service.
Probation Revocation – Court proceeding to revoke suspended sentence or probation upon violation of supervision conditions.
Parole – Conditional supervised release from incarceration prior to completion of full sentence; subject to Board of Corrections approval.
entries.statuteTitle: Parole Act / Board of Corrections Rules

entries.description: Parole Revocation – Administrative or judicial proceeding to revoke parole upon violation of release conditions.
entries.statuteTitle: Parole Act / Board of Corrections Rules

entries.description: Good Time / Earned Time Credits – Sentence reduction mechanism for compliant institutional behavior; reduces time served toward parole eligibility.
Community Correction Center Transfer – Residential placement in a community correction center as alternative to or transition from state prison incarceration.

Escape / Absconding from Supervision – Criminal charge for willfully failing to return to custody or absconding from supervised release conditions; penal code track; charged as a standalone criminal offense.

Supervision Revocation Procedure – Administrative and judicial process for revoking probation, parole, or community supervision upon violation of conditions; criminal procedure track; not a standalone criminal charge but appears on court dockets as a revocation proceeding.
entries.statuteTitle: Arkansas Community Punishment Act / Criminal Procedure

entries.description: Electronic Monitoring – Court- or board-ordered GPS/electronic monitoring as a condition of release, supervision, or alternative to incarceration.
Work Release – Supervised employment program allowing incarcerated individuals to work in the community during daytime hours while returning to facility nightly.
Work Release Violation – Failure to return to facility, job loss, or other breach of work release conditions.
Record Sealing – Statutory process to seal (restrict public access to) eligible criminal records after completion of sentence and applicable waiting period.
entries.statuteTitle: Comprehensive Criminal Record Sealing Act of 2013

entries.description: Expungement – Process to physically destroy or set aside eligible criminal records; more complete than sealing; treated as if the offense never occurred for most purposes.
Governor's Pardon – Executive clemency restoring civil rights and forgiving the offense; does not expunge the record but removes legal disqualifications associated with the conviction.
entries.statuteNumber: Ark. Const. art. 6, § 18 / Ark. Code Ann. § 16-93-207

entries.description: Restoration of Voting Rights – Process by which persons convicted of felonies regain the right to vote upon completion of sentence including any supervision period.
entries.statuteNumber: Ark. Const. art. 3, § 2 / Ark. Code Ann. § 16-93-614

entries.description: Restoration of Firearm Rights – State and federal processes for restoring the right to possess firearms after a felony conviction; state restoration does not automatically restore federal rights.
Juvenile Record Sealing – Automatic or petition-based sealing of juvenile court adjudication records; prevents juvenile record from appearing on standard adult background checks.
DNA Record Expungement – Process to expunge DNA records from the Arkansas State DNA Database upon acquittal, dismissal, or reversal of conviction.
Effect of Sealed Record on Employment – Statutory provisions governing what employers can and cannot ask or access regarding sealed criminal records.
entries.statuteTitle: Comprehensive Criminal Record Sealing Act of 2013

entries.description: Administrative Record Correction – Process to correct factual errors in criminal history records maintained by Arkansas Crime Information Center (ACIC) or other state databases.
entries.statuteNumber: Ark. Code Ann. § 16-90-1401 / Admin. Procedure Act

entries.description: Employer Access to Sealed Records – Statutory framework governing which employers and agencies retain lawful access to sealed criminal records notwithstanding sealing order.
entries.statuteTitle: Comprehensive Criminal Record Sealing Act of 2013

entries.description: Certificate of Rehabilitation – Court-issued document demonstrating rehabilitation; may mitigate collateral consequences for employment and licensing even where full expungement is unavailable.
Reckless Driving – Driving in willful or wanton disregard for the safety of persons or property.

entries.subcategory: Hit and Run — Property Damage Only (Misdemeanor)
entries.description: Leaving Scene of Accident (Hit and Run) – Failing to stop and remain at the scene of an accident involving injury or death.
Fleeing by Vehicle – Purposely fleeing in a motor vehicle from a law enforcement officer signaling to stop.
Implied Consent / Refusal – Refusing to submit to a chemical test of breath, blood, or urine for DWI detection.
entries.statuteTitle: Omnibus Driving Under the Influence Act

entries.description: Drug Paraphernalia – Possessing items to plant, propagate, cultivate, harvest, pack, store, or inject drugs.
Maintaining Drug Premises – Knowingly keeping/maintaining a structure, vehicle, or facility used for drug activities.
Simultaneous Possession – Possessing a firearm while simultaneously possessing a felony quantity of drugs.
Doctor Shopping / Rx Fraud – Obtaining controlled substances by fraud, deceit, misrepresentation, or concealment.
Counterfeit Substances – Creating, delivering, or possessing with intent to deliver a counterfeit substance.
Meth Lab Equipment – Possessing equipment, products, or materials with intent to manufacture methamphetamine; items include pseudoephedrine, glassware, tubing, or other lab apparatus.

Precursor Chemicals (Anhydrous Ammonia) – Possessing anhydrous ammonia with intent to manufacture methamphetamine; includes unlawful storage, transport, or transfer of anhydrous ammonia in non-approved containers.
Drug-Free Zone Enhancements – Manufacturing, delivering, or possessing drugs within 1,000 feet of schools, parks, or youth centers.
Delivery to Minors – An adult delivering a controlled substance to a minor who is at least 3 years younger.
Fentanyl Enhancements – Mandatory sentencing enhancements for trafficking or causing death with fentanyl.
Domestic Battering – Purposely causing physical injury to a family or household member (1st, 2nd, 3rd Degree).

Aggravated Assault on a Family or Household Member – Purposely engaging in conduct, displaying a firearm, or strangling/impeding respiration under circumstances manifesting extreme indifference to human life.
Strangulation Enhancements – Impeding normal breathing or circulation by applying pressure to the throat or neck.

entries.subcategory: Endangering the Welfare of a Minor
entries.description: Endangering the welfare of a minor in the first degree – Knowingly engaging in conduct creating a substantial risk of death or serious physical injury to a minor, or causing or permitting a minor to be exposed to a controlled substance.
