entries.description: Discharging unpermitted sewage, industrial waste, or other pollutants into waters of the state without an active National Pollutant Discharge Elimination System (NPDES) permit.
Administrative determination of non-compliance resulting in the assessment of civil financial penalties and cost liability for damages to the state's natural resources.
Knowingly operating, delivering petroleum product into, or maintaining an underground storage tank system without a valid compliance certificate from the state.
Intentionally falsifying engineering soil data, water sample records, or remediation reports submitted under the Tennessee Voluntary Cleanup and Property Redevelopment Act.
Operating a solid waste processing or disposal facility/hauling operation without a valid permit, or illegal dumping of commercial solid waste on unapproved public/private land.
Performing asbestos abatement, encapsulation, or removal operations in public or commercial facilities without holding an active, unrevoked state asbestos contractor accreditation.
Manufacturing, processing, packing, or holding food under unsanitary conditions whereby it may have become contaminated with filth or rendered injurious to public health.
Knowingly selling, giving, or furnishing alcoholic beverages to any person under twenty-one (21) years of age.
Knowingly selling, furnishing, giving, or causing to be sold, furnished, or given, any alcoholic beverage, wine, or beer to any person under twenty-one (21) years of age.

Operating a retail food establishment, commercial kitchen, mobile food unit, or catering facility without securing or maintaining an active public health permit from the state or local county health department.

Operating a substance abuse treatment facility, non-residential opioid treatment program (OTP), detoxification unit, or sober living home without holding an active facility license from the Department of Mental Health and Substance Abuse Services.

Operating a residential or community-based substance abuse treatment facility, methadone clinic, or Office-Based Opiate Treatment (OBOT) program without a valid license from the Department of Mental Health and Substance Abuse Services (DMHSAS).

Knowingly manufacturing, delivering, selling, or possessing with intent to manufacture, deliver, or sell a controlled substance classified under Schedules I through VII of the Tennessee Controlled Substances Act.

Acquiring or obtaining possession of a controlled substance by misrepresentation, fraud, forgery, deception, subterfuge, or the use of a fraudulent DEA registration or prescription pad (commonly including clinician "doctor shopping" or prescription diversion).

Dispensing, distributing, prescribing, or administering a controlled substance by a practitioner or entity without holding a valid, active state license, pharmacy registration, or specialized DEA certificate.
Manufacturing, processing, packing, holding, or selling food products that are adulterated, decomposed, contaminated with filth, or prepared under unsanitary conditions endangering public health.
Knowingly presenting, or causing to be presented, a false or fraudulent claim for payment or approval under the Medicaid/TennCare program.
Knowingly and willfully making or causing to be made any false statement or representation of a material fact in any application for TennCare benefits or provider payments.

Statutory mandate requiring Tennessee health boards to enact suspension or revocation against any practitioner who has been excluded, suspended, or terminated from an out-of-state Medicaid program (e.g., an Arkansas Medicaid Exclusion).

State statutory alignment mechanism requiring immediate termination of employment or billing capability for any individual found on the Federal OIG List of Excluded Individuals/Entities (LEIE) for healthcare fraud or patient neglect.
Statutory mandate compelling all state licensing boards and medical malpractice insurers to report any adverse licensing actions, malpractice payments, or clinical privilege restrictions to the NPDB.
Soliciting, receiving, offering, or paying any kickback, bribe, or rebate for referring an individual for TennCare/Medicaid covered services.
Conspiring with one or more persons to get a false or fraudulent claim allowed or paid under the TennCare/Medicaid program.
Preparing or presenting false, incomplete, or misleading material statements in support of a claim for payment or benefit under an insurance policy.
Administrative regulation empowering the Bureau of TennCare to execute summary suspension of billing lines and immediate recoupment of funds based on a credible allegation of provider fraud.

Acting as a residential roofing services provider without proper state registration, or violating statutory rules regarding insurance deductible offsets and consumer contract parameters.
Operating any elevator, moving walk, or escalator for public use without an active certificate of operation issued by the Department of Labor and Workforce Development.

Retaining or misapplying construction loan draws, multi-family down payments, or commercial project funds intended for sub-contractors, laborers, or material suppliers.
Operating a commercial enterprise installing fire, burglar, or safety tracking alarm systems, or providing security services without an active specialty company license from the state.

Engaging in unprofessional, dishonorable, or unethical conduct, or demonstrating gross malpractice or persistent negligence in medical practice.

Obtaining or attempting to procure a nursing license through fraud, deceit, or material misrepresentation, or engaging in narcotics diversion or active chemical impairment during clinical shift tracking.
Violating state pharmacy compounding standards, dispensing toxic alterations, or failing to maintain drug safety tracking protocols under the Board of Pharmacy.

Intentionally misclassifying workforce members as independent contractors, making false statements regarding payroll records, or hiding corporate payroll structures to evade mandatory state unemployment fund payments.

Developing an appraisal report containing fraud, deceit, or gross negligence, or violating Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice (USPAP) guidelines to artificially alter real property valuation calculations.

Operating an unpermitted shop, practicing cosmetology, barbering, or aesthetics without an active state license, or maintaining severe, repeated health/sanitation violations.
Charging extortionate premium rates, soliciting business inside local jail facilities, failing to maintain statutory capital reserves, or employing convicted felons as runners.
Making willful or substantial misrepresentations, failing to timely account for or remit escrow funds, practicing undisclosed dual agency, or accepting commissions outside the principal broker.
Engaging in attorney professional misconduct, including co-mingling or stealing client trust funds (IOLTA fraud), severe neglect of client legal matters, or felony criminal tracking.
entries.statuteTitle: Rules of the Supreme Court of the State of Tennessee

Engaging in false or misleading advertising, using 'shills' or fake bidders to artificially drive up property prices, or failing to properly maintain, separate, and timely account for escrow or consignment sales funds belonging to others.
Unlawfully soliciting bail bond business inside a local jail facility, courthouse, or police precinct, or paying kickbacks to jailers or police personnel for client referrals.
Failing to maintain statutory capital reserves, failing to pay final judgments on forfeited bonds, or misapplying consumer collateral assets held in trust.
Submitting fraudulent or intentionally fabricated investigation reports to clients, executing illegal wiretapping, or engaging in unpermitted GPS tracking.
Knowingly notarizing a forged signature, executing a false certificate, or certifying an acknowledgment without the signing party physically present before the officer.
Intentionally altering or falsifying an official judicial deposition or trial transcript, or practicing court reporting without holding an active state license.

Utilizing fraudulent or deceptive collection mechanisms, violating the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA) parameters systematically, or commingling client recovery pools with corporate operating reserves.
Operating an unpermitted cosmetology, barber, or aesthetics shop, or demonstrating repeated, severe health and sanitation infractions creating bloodborne pathogen exposure risks to the public.
Acts or omissions violating the Rules of Professional Conduct, conviction of a serious crime, or willful refusal to comply with a court order.

Knowingly purchasing scrap metal products or catalytic converters without verifying identification, failing to maintain transactional tracking databases, or paying in cash to evade state verification laws.

Defrauding any buyer or selling party, making intentional misrepresentations regarding vehicle structural history, or engaging in illegal odometer rolling/tampering to inflate commercial asset metrics.

Accepting, giving or charging any undisclosed rebate, kickback, or unearned profit on expenditures made for a principal in a real estate transaction.
Procuring or attempting to procure an appraiser registration, license, or certification by making false statements, falsifying logs, or hiding disqualifying metrics.

Utilizing fraudulent diagnostic parameters to sell unneeded medical hearing hardware, bait-and-switch advertising patterns targeting elder consumers, or practicing without a valid license.

Failure to Report Child Abuse — A second or subsequent violation of failing to report child abuse, or any single instance where the person intentionally fails to make the required report of child abuse.
Minor Administrative Violation — Any technical, facility, or paperwork standard violation that is not explicitly designated as a major violation or a safety risk under department protocols.

entries.description: Stacking Major Violation — Having six (6) or more minor violations of any type within a 3-month block, or three (3) or more minor violations of the exact same regulation within a 3-month block.
entries.statuteTitle: Rules of the Tennessee Department of Human Services (Family Assistance Division)

entries.description: Failure to Report Clinical Privilege Restrictions — Failure by a chief medical officer, medical entity, or peer review committee to report any restriction, suspension, or surrender of a physician's clinical privileges lasting over 30 days due to competence or professional misconduct.

Failure to Report Malpractice Claim Data — Failure of an insurance entity, risk retention group, or self-insured healthcare provider to electronically transmit comprehensive malpractice claim files, dispositions, and settlement values to the state database.
entries.statuteTitle: Rules of the Tennessee Department of Commerce and Insurance (Insurance Division)

entries.description: Violation of Affirmative Reporting Mandate — Every person having reasonable cause to suspect that a child has been subjected to child abuse or neglect must report that knowledge or suspicion immediately. This specific section creates the core universal mandatory legal duty.

Failure to Report Child Abuse (Baseline Misdemeanor) — Knowingly failing to provide immediate notification to the Department of Children's Services or local law enforcement when there is a reasonable suspicion of child abuse or neglect.
Malicious False Reporting — Knowingly making a false report of child abuse, sexual abuse, or neglect with malice, intent to defame, or intent to manipulate legal or domestic proceedings.

Intentionally Withholding or Falsifying Information — Intentionally making false statements, misrepresenting staff credentials, or withholding material safety information during child care license applications or state inspections.

Inspection Obstruction & Safety Plan Violation — Blocking, delaying, or refusing entry to DHS inspectors, or willfully failing to execute an active emergency child-safety plan ordered by the department.

Negligent Injury Violation — Any violation of agency safety, staffing, or structural standards that directly results in injury, medical emergency, or physical harm to a child under the facility's supervision.
entries.statuteTitle: Rules of the Tennessee Department of Human Services (Family Assistance Division)

entries.description: Tennessee Identity Theft Deterrence and Credit Protection Act. Unlawful deployment, misuse, or failure to secure consumer credit reports, security freezes, and personal identifying information (PII).
Statutory cross-activation of a Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (FINRA) bar or suspension for failing to deliver information, books, or records requested under FINRA Rule 8210.
Engaging in acts, practices, or courses of business by an investment adviser that operate as fraud, deceit, or an explicit breach of the core fiduciary duty to a client.

Adoption of an active Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) administrative sanction order finding serious, systemic violations of federal securities rules (e.g., Securities Act of 1933, Investment Advisers Act of 1940).
Serious violations of the Rules of Professional Conduct (including conversion of client trust funds, perjury, or systemic unauthorized practice of law).
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Supreme Court Rules / Tennessee Code Annotated

entries.description: Residential or commercial contracting violations, including operating beyond license monetary ceilings, safety infractions, or failure to secure and maintain a valid surety bond.
Insurance Producer Fraud and Misrepresentation; committing an unfair trade practice, fraud, or intentional material misrepresentation in the solicitation or sale of an insurance policy.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Rules of Tennessee Department of Commerce and Insurance

entries.description: Unlicensed Real Estate Brokerage / Broker Misrepresentation; acting as a real estate broker, affiliate broker, or timeshare salesperson without a valid license, or engaging in flagrant misrepresentation while licensed.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Real Estate Broker License Act

entries.description: Uncertified Corporate Trade Practice / Corporate Impersonation of Regulated Fields; operating an entity or holding out an entity to practice law, medicine, securities sales, or regulated commercial contracting without state board certification.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Consumer Protection Act / Criminal Unauthorized Practice Control

entries.description: Failure to Provide Information / FINRA Non-Compliance Bar; failing to provide testimony, documents, or written responses requested by FINRA during an investigation, triggering an automatic cross-jurisdictional state-level revocation.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Securities Act of 1980 / FINRA Code of Procedure

entries.description: SEC Administrative Cease-and-Desist Enforcement; being subject to an order of the Securities and Exchange Commission denying, suspending, or revoking registration, creating an automatic basis for state disciplinary enforcement.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Securities Act / Federal Cross-Adoption Framework

entries.description: Unauthorized Practice of Medicine; practicing medicine, surgery, or obstetrics without a license from the Board of Medical Examiners, or holding oneself out as a licensed physician.

Illegal Corporate Practice of Medicine; a non-physician owned business entity or corporate structure employing physicians to practice medicine or controlling clinical decision-making without satisfying statutory exemptions (e.g., licensed hospitals or designated clinics).
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Medical Practice Act / Corporate Form Control

entries.description: Commercial Fee-Splitting and Medical Rebating; a physician dividing fees, offering rebates, or paying kickbacks to an unlicensed individual or corporate entity in exchange for patient referrals.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Medical Practice Act / Commercial Bribery Matrix

entries.description: Unlicensed Pharmacy Operation or Drug Wholesaling; operating a pharmacy, outsourcing facility, or wholesale drug distribution facility without an active license from the Tennessee Board of Pharmacy.
Wholesale Drug Diversion and Peddling; knowingly purchasing, selling, or transferring prescription drugs via unauthorized pedigree layouts or from uncertified secondary wholesalers.

Unlicensed Chiropractic Practice / Scope Exceedance; practicing chiropractic adjustments, spinal manipulation, or holding oneself out as a chiropractor without an active license from the Board of Chiropractic Examiners.

Unauthorized Practice of Professional or Practical Nursing; practicing as an RN, LPN, or Advanced Practice Registered Nurse (APRN), or utilizing matching credentials without an active license from the Tennessee Board of Nursing.

Boating Under the Influence (BUI) First Offense; operating any vessel, motorboat, personal watercraft (PWC), or sailboat on any public or state waters while under the influence of an intoxicant, marijuana, narcotic drug, or substance affecting the central nervous system, or with a blood/breath alcohol concentration (BAC) of 0.08% or greater.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Navigation Safety Controls

entries.description: Boating Implied Consent Violation; refusing to submit to a chemical test (blood, breath, or urine) for the purpose of determining alcohol or drug content when requested by a law enforcement officer or TWRA agent with reasonable cause.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Implied Consent Regulations

entries.description: Aggravated Boating Under the Influence / Vehicular Assault by Vessel; operating a watercraft while intoxicated and proximately causing serious bodily injury to another person during a collision, wreck, or erratic navigation maneuver.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Vehicular Assault Extrapolation

entries.description: Boating Under the Influence (BUI) Second Offense; operating a vessel or watercraft on state or public waters while intoxicated, having one prior BUI or DUI conviction within a 10-year lookback period.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Navigation Safety Controls

entries.description: Boating Under the Influence (BUI) Third or Subsequent Offense; operating any vessel or watercraft while under the influence of alcohol, narcotics, or central nervous system depressants, with two or more prior BUI or DUI convictions within a 10-year lookback window.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Navigation Safety Controls

entries.description: Boating Under the Influence with a Minor Passenger; operating any vessel or watercraft while intoxicated on public or state waters while accompanied by a passenger under the age of eighteen (18).
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Child Protection Frameworks

entries.description: Vehicular Homicide by Vessel (BUI Felony Homicide); the reckless killing of another person as the proximate result of operating a vessel, watercraft, or motorboat while under the influence of an intoxicant, drug, or marijuana.

Aggravated Boating Under the Influence; operating any vessel, motorboat, or watercraft in a reckless, negligent, or erratic manner while under the influence of an intoxicant, and directly causing a multi-vessel collision, structural disaster, or serious bodily injury to another individual.

Operating Unregistered Commercial Watercraft; operating, or giving permission for the operation of, any commercial passenger-carrying vessel, charter vessel, or commercial shipping watercraft on state or public waters without an active Certificate of Number or valid registration.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Identification Numbers for Vessels

entries.description: Commercial Fleet Safety Failure / Passenger-for-Hire Equipment Violation; failure of a commercial watercraft fleet operator, passenger boat, or livery operator to maintain state-mandated life-saving equipment, structural hulls, fire suppression devices, or U.S. Coast Guard-approved safety modifications.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Boating Safety Regulations

entries.description: Knowing Authorization of Illegal Vessel Operation; an owner or person directly in charge of a watercraft knowingly authorizing or permitting the vessel to be operated on state waters in direct violation of state registration, safety, or licensing mandates.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Boating Safety Liability

entries.description: Altering or Forging State Lottery Tickets; knowingly influencing, altering, counterfeiting, or forging a lottery ticket issued under the Tennessee Education Lottery Corporation framework for the purpose of deceptive entry or financial gain.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Education Lottery Implementation Law

entries.description: Uttering or Presenting a Forged Lottery Ticket; knowingly presenting, uttering, or attempting to redeem an altered, counterfeited, or forged lottery ticket or prize claim to the lottery corporation, a retail agent, or state authority for monetary payout.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Education Lottery Implementation Law

entries.description: Insider Lottery Manipulation / Employee Lottery Fraud; an officer, employee, contractor, or retail operator of the Tennessee Education Lottery Corporation knowingly rigging data feeds, pre-determining winning draw patterns, or falsifying corporate operational metrics for private profit.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Education Lottery Implementation Law

entries.description: Lottery Fiduciary Defalcation and Conversion; failure of a certified lottery retailer or its corporate officers to preserve, account for, and remit ticket sales proceeds, treating public ticket revenues as a personal or corporate slush fund.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Lottery Retailer Fiduciary Obligations

entries.description: Criminal Simulation of Gaming Security Markers; making or altering an object (such as scratch-off ticket bar codes, security foils, or thermal print markers) so that it appears to have an authenticity, rarity, or source value that it does not possess.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Offenses Against Property

entries.description: Forgery of Financial Instruments; forging, executing, or uttering any written state instrument or prize assignment document with the intent to defraud, where the value of the target instrument dictates the final felony grade matching standard theft ladders.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Offenses Against Property

entries.description: Critical Infrastructure Interference (Port Facility); knowingly interrupting, interfering with, destroying, or injuring the operations or infrastructure of railroads, commercial maritime shipping channels, river ports, loading docks, or public transport terminals.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Offenses Against Property

entries.description: Breach of Port Authority Directives; violating lawful safety orders, berthing constraints, hazardous cargo positioning layouts, or maritime safety rules established by a regional or municipal Tennessee Port Authority.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Port Authorities and Basins

entries.description: Restricted Maritime Security Boundary Breach; entering or remaining within a designated maritime secure zone, deepwater commercial harbor perimeter, or safety zone established under the Maritime Transportation Security Act (MTSA) without authorization.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Federal Ports and Waterways Safety General Cross-Adoption

entries.description: Public Property and Port Obstruction; establishing unapproved structures, blocking, or unauthorized camping/occupying on public property, state loading docks, or river port roadways, effectively obstructing transit access lines to regulated shipping infrastructure.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Equal Access to Public Property Act

entries.description: Violation of Regional Port Authority Structural Mandate; establishing unapproved docks, altering channels, or erecting marine obstructions within the geographic boundary of a certified Tennessee Port Authority without active engineering permits.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Regional Port Authorities

entries.description: Criminal Trespass on Restricted Infrastructure; entering or remaining on any restricted, fenced, or clearly posted maritime facility, river port loading deck, or harbor storage terminal without the explicit consent of the property owner or port supervisor.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Offenses Against Property

entries.description: Failure to Confine a Dog / Animal At Large; allowing a dog or animal under one's control to go uncontrolled upon the premises of another without consent, or upon a highway, public road, street, or any other place open to the public generally.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Dogs Regulated; Liability for Injuries

entries.description: Failure to Confine a Vicious Dog Resulting in Injury; an owner or keeper failing to maintain containment of a known vicious or dangerous stray animal, directly resulting in the animal attacking and causing bodily injury to a human being off the owner's property.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Dogs Regulated; Liability for Injuries

entries.description: Aggravated Cruelty to Animals; knowingly and brutally torturing, crushing, starving, or maiming a captive dangerous stray, feral animal, or companion animal, or unreasonably withholding sustenance resulting in serious physical injury or death.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Offenses Against Property

entries.description: Defiance of Stray Animal Rabies Quarantine; harboring or concealing a stray or feral animal that has bitten a human being, or refusing to surrender the animal to public health authorities or animal control for a mandatory 10-day clinical observation and quarantine period.

Refusal to Remediate Public Health Hazards; failure or explicit refusal of a real property owner or custodian to clear, clean, or remediate a parcel or building officially designated as a chronic public health hazard, environmental menace, or vermin harboring point following statutory county notice.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / County Authority to Clear Vegetation and Hazardous Waste

entries.description: Uniform Building Code Non-Compliance; constructing, altering, or maintaining a commercial, multi-family, or residential building in direct defiance of safety regulations established by the State Fire Marshal, creating life-safety defects, structural fire traps, or unsafe exit-way configurations.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Safety and Building Standards

entries.subcategory: Willful Vandalism of Public Infrastructure / State Buildings
entries.description: Vandalism of Public Infrastructure / State Buildings; knowingly causing damage, defacement, structural sabotage, or destruction to real or personal property owned by the state, political subdivisions, river port authorities, state loading docks, or harbor authority complexes.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Offenses Against Property

entries.description: Gambling Fraud / Casino Cheating; winning or attempting to win money or property via a game of chance or wagering track through the use of loaded dice, marked cards, cheating devices, sleight of hand, or fraudulent digital manipulation of electronic gaming kiosks.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Offenses Against Property

entries.description: Gambling Promotion / Racetrack Bookmaking; knowingly inducing or enticing persons to engage in illegal gambling, or establishing, maintaining, or operating a pool, lottery, racetrack bookmaking ring, or pari-mutuel horse wagering matrix for private or syndicate profit.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Offenses Against Public Health, Safety, and Welfare

entries.description: Seizure and Forfeiture of Gambling Devices and Proceeds; the statutory seizure and permanent forfeiture to the state of Tennessee of all money, electronic gaming machines, poker tables, communications equipment, vehicles, and real property used in or derived from an illegal gambling operation.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Offenses Against Public Health, Safety, and Welfare

entries.description: Unlawful Gambling Participation; knowingly making or accepting a bet, playing an unauthorized game of chance for money or property, or participating in an un-permitted lottery scheme as an individual participant.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Offenses Against Public Health, Safety, and Welfare

entries.description: Unlawful Gambling Operation; owning, managing, or operating a non-permitted game of chance, slot machine ring, commercial dice game, or internet gambling server for commercial profit.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Offenses Against Public Health, Safety, and Welfare

entries.description: Possession of a Gambling Device or Record; knowingly manufacturing, selling, transporting, or possessing any electronic gaming machine, slot machine, video poker apparatus, or written bookkeeping record intended to be utilized in an illegal gambling operation.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Offenses Against Public Health, Safety, and Welfare

entries.description: Sports Wagering Regulatory Violation; failure of a licensed mobile sports wagering operator or corporate entity to implement required geolocation tracking blocks, verify minor exclusions, preserve bettor logs, or accurately report state privilege tax metrics.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Sports Gaming Act Framework

entries.description: Participant Sports Rigging Enhancement; an active professional or collegiate athlete, coach, official, or trainer knowingly accepting a bribe, intentionally throwing a match, altering points, or manipulating performance metrics to influence the outcome of a publicly exhibited contest for gambling/wagering exploitation.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Offenses Against Property

entries.description: Judicial Padlocking of a Gambling House Nuisance; a civil chancery court decree ordering the immediate locking, sealing, and physical padlocking of an entire building, commercial space, or real property parcel found to be hosting systemic, illicit gambling promotions or unauthorized lottery networks.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Abatement of Nuisances Generally

entries.description: Possession of Commercial Gambling Devices / Records; knowingly manufacturing, selling, transporting, or possessing any slot machine, electronic video lottery terminal, or physical bookmaking betting records with the intent to operate or facilitate commercial gambling promotion.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Offenses Against Public Health, Safety and Welfare

entries.description: Possession of Gambling Records for Profit Promotion; possessing, writing, or collecting numbers, slips, tokens, or digital logs designed to record bets or wagers with the explicit intent to profit from managing or promoting a policy game, unauthorized pool, or lottery ring.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Offenses Against Public Health, Safety and Welfare

entries.subcategory: Financial Exploitation by Power of Attorney / Guardian
entries.description: Financial Exploitation by Power of Attorney; knowingly using a power of attorney instrument to deceive, intimidate, convert, or deplete the cash, real estate, personal property, or assets of an elderly (60+) or vulnerable adult principal for personal or corporate gain.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Offenses Against the Family

entries.description: Caregiver Financial Exploitation via Undue Influence; knowingly and through undue influence, deception, coercion, or fraud, converting or depleting the financial resources, real estate holdings, or liquid assets of an elderly or vulnerable adult without valid legal instruments like a POA.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Offenses Against the Family

entries.description: Misappropriation of Ward Assets by Conservator/Guardian; a court-appointed conservator or guardian knowingly converting, embezzling, or misappropriating the real estate, cash, or investment assets of a minor or disabled ward for personal or third-party use in violation of court-mandated management parameters.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Guardianship and Conservatorship Management

entries.description: Fiduciary Accounting Non-Compliance; failure or explicit refusal of a court-appointed guardian, conservator, or administrator to file required annual financial accountings, inventory sheets, or bank statements regarding a ward's real and personal estate after explicit probate court citation.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Guardianship and Conservatorship Management

entries.description: Tenant Failure to Pay Rent / Lease Non-Compliance; failure of a residential tenant to pay rent within the contractually defined grace period or violating core behavioral/lease terms, triggering a statutory 14-day notice to remediate or vacate under URLTA parameters.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Uniform Residential Landlord and Tenant Act (URLTA)

entries.description: Execution of a Writ of Possession; the mechanical sheriff-enforced physical clearing of real property, removal of a tenant, and restoration of possession to the property owner following a final judicial FED decree and the expiration of the statutory 10-day appeal window.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Forcible Entry and Detainer (FED)

entries.description: Deed Fraud and Title Simulation; knowingly making, forging, altering, or uttering a fraudulent real estate deed, property conveyance instrument, or estate title assignment with the intent to record it with a county register of deeds to fraudulently convert property ownership.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Offenses Against Property

entries.description: Unauthorized Directional Subsurface Drilling and Unlawful Pooling; executing directional drilling, horizontal extraction, or subsurface pooling of oil, gas, or minerals across real property boundary lines without an active state permit, mineral easement, or valid pooling order.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Oil and Gas Production Regulations

entries.description: Unlicensed Banking Operation; conducting a banking business, advertising or holding an entity out as a bank, or receiving deposits without a valid state charter or explicit written certificate of authority from the Commissioner of Financial Institutions.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Banking Act / Regulation of Banks and Trust Companies

entries.description: False Banking Book Entries; an officer, director, employee, or agent of a state-chartered bank or trust company knowingly making false entries in bank books, falsifying reports, or misrepresenting financial metrics to deceive TDFI examiners or examiners from the Federal Reserve/FDIC.

Receipt of Deposits During Insolvency; an officer, director, or employee of a bank receiving or permitting the receipt of public deposits when they know or have clear reason to know that the financial institution is insolvent.

Unlicensed Money Transmission; engaging in the business of money transmission, issuing payment instruments, storing value cards, or transferring currency digitally or physically without an active license issued by the Commissioner of Financial Institutions.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Money Transmitter Act / Licensing Requirements

entries.description: Unlicensed Consumer Lending / Industrial Loan Violations; engaging in the business of making consumer loans, equity-linked loans, or charging interest rates exceeding standard state usury caps without an active license registered under the Industrial Loan and Thrift Act framework.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Industrial Loan and Thrift Companies Act / Prohibited Acts

entries.description: TDFI Civil Money Penalty Assessment; a state-level final order levying non-criminal monetary penalties against a bank or trust company director for violating a final cease-and-desist order, maintaining unsafe or unsound lending concentrations, or ignoring prompt corrective action warnings.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Department of Financial Institutions / Administration and Enforcement

entries.description: Administrative Non-Compliance / Violation of TDFI Safety and Soundness Rules; violating a written cease-and-desist order, operating with critically deficient capital structures, failing to comply with prompt corrective action mandates, or submitting false reports to the Commissioner of Financial Institutions.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Department of Financial Institutions Act

entries.description: Personal Fiduciary Liability Assessment of Bank Directors; a state-ordered civil surcharge or personal financial assessment levied directly against a bank director or executive officer for gross negligence, intentional violation of lending limits, or permitting unsafe insider loan practices that result in loss to the institution.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Banking Institutions Act

entries.description: Misapplication of Bank Funds by Fiduciary Officer; an officer, director, employee, or agent of a state-chartered bank or trust company knowingly embezzling, abstracting, misapplying, or willfully diverting any of the bank's currency, funds, assets, or credits with the intent to injure or defraud the institution.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Banking Institutions Act

entries.description: Unregistered Broker-Dealer or Agent Operations; transacting business in Tennessee as a broker-dealer, agent, investment adviser, or investment adviser representative without maintaining an active registration with the Tennessee Securities Division.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Securities Act of 1980

entries.description: Sale of Unregistered Securities; selling or offering to sell any security in the State of Tennessee unless it has an active state registration, is a federally covered security, or qualifies for a lawful exemption.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Securities Act of 1980

entries.description: Employment of Fraudulent Securities Devices; employing any device, scheme, or artifice to defraud, making material misrepresentations, or engaging in a course of business that operates as a fraud or deceit upon any person in connection with the offer, sale, or purchase of a security.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Securities Act of 1980

entries.description: Market Manipulation and Wash Sales; creating a false or misleading appearance of active trading in any security, or false appearance with respect to the market for any such security, via wash sales, matched orders, or artificial volume simulation for the purpose of manipulating asset prices.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Securities Act of 1980

entries.description: Reciprocal Statutory Disqualification Stop-Order; being subject to an order, adjudication, or suspension by another state securities administrator, the SEC, FINRA, or a foreign regulatory authority, triggering an automatic reciprocal state stop-order or license revocation in Tennessee.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Securities Act of 1980

entries.description: Controlling Person Civil Securities Liability; every person who directly or indirectly controls a seller or issuer liable for securities fraud, every partner, officer, or director of such seller, and every broker-dealer or agent who materially aids in the act, being held jointly and severally liable to the investor.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Securities Act of 1980

entries.description: Reciprocal Penny Stock Enforcement Bar; being subject to a federal Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) order revoking registration or barring association under the Penny Stock Reform Act, triggering an automatic cross-jurisdictional license revocation in Tennessee.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Securities Act of 1980

entries.description: Securities Record-Keeping Defiance / Anti-Shredding Violation; willfully failing to maintain mandatory blotters, ledgers, and customer account records, or altering, shredding, concealing, or falsifying broker-dealer accounts to obstruct a lawful state regulatory audit or examination.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Securities Act of 1980

entries.description: Corporate Officer and Director Securities Bar; an administrative order issued by the Commissioner denying, suspending, or revoking an individual's capacity to serve as an officer, director, partner, or principal of any registered issuer or broker-dealer based on prior fraudulent market practices.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Securities Act of 1980

entries.description: Policy Terms Misrepresentation; making, issuing, or circulating any estimate, illustration, circular, or statement misrepresenting the terms, benefits, or advantages of any insurance policy issued or to be issued by a licensed underwriter.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Unfair Trade Practices and Consumer Protection

entries.description: Twisting; making any misrepresentation or incomplete comparison of policies to any person for the purpose of inducing, or tending to induce, the person to lapse, forfeit, surrender, retain, exchange, or convert any insurance policy.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Unfair Trade Practices and Consumer Protection

entries.description: Systemic Unfair Claims Settlement Practices; executing general business practices that include misrepresenting pertinent policy facts, refusing to pay claims without conducting a reasonable investigation, failing to affirm or deny coverage within a reasonable time, or compelling insureds to institute litigation to recover amounts due.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Unfair Trade Practices and Consumer Protection

entries.description: Statutory Insurance Bad Faith Civil Penalty; an insurance company refusing to pay a loss within sixty (60) days after a demand has been made by the holder of the policy, where such refusal is judicially determined to be not in good faith.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Insurance Policies Generally

entries.description: Fraudulent Asset Transfer Prior to Insolvency; an officer, director, or parent entity executing transfers of an insurance company's assets, reserves, or real property within 1 year prior to the filing of a petition for rehabilitation or liquidation, with the intent to defraud, delay, or hinder policyholders, creditors, or the Commissioner.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Insurers Rehabilitation and Liquidation Act

entries.description: Reciprocal Out-of-State Asset Clawback Violation; an out-of-state insurer or holding company failing to comply with an ancillary receiver's demand or court order in Tennessee to return localized assets, premium reserves, or property belonging to a domiciliary insurer undergoing liquidation in another state.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Insurers Rehabilitation and Liquidation Act

entries.description: Statutory Scope and Definition of Fraudulent Insurance Acts; establishing the specific legal boundaries for masking, concealment, or systemic accounting deceptions within an insurer's capital structures, which serves as the predicate trigger for multi-agency enforcement overlays.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Insurance Fraud Actions

entries.description: Fraudulent Concealment from Insurance Commissioner; knowingly hiding, masking, or failing to disclose material financial or structural operational details requested by the Commissioner of Commerce and Insurance during an inspection, audit, or rehabilitation review.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Insurance Fraud Actions

entries.description: Unlicensed Practice of Public Accountancy; performing attest or compilation services, or using the titles "Certified Public Accountant," "CPA," "Public Accountant," or any abbreviations intended to deceive the public into believing the individual or firm holds an active certificate issued by the Tennessee State Board of Accountancy.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Public Accountancy Act

entries.description: Accountant Falsification of Financial Statements / Auditing Forgery; knowingly making, certifying, or presenting a false financial report, balance sheet, or audit statement, or willfully altering corporate ledger entries to mask institutional asset defalcation, evade state/federal revenue tracking, or inflate public corporate metrics.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Public Accountancy Act Integration

entries.description: Accountancy Confidentiality Breach / Board Investigative Obstruction; willfully disclosing confidential client financial data without authorization outside statutory legal vectors, or refusing to supply mandatory audit workpapers, records, or testimony requested by the TBA during a formal regulatory discipline or fraud check.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Public Accountancy Act

entries.description: Peer Review Deception and Non-Compliance; a licensed accountancy firm or managing partner failing to undergo required state-mandated peer reviews, or knowingly submitting falsified, altered, or deceptive peer review compliance documentation to the Board to preserve firm permit certifications.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Public Accountancy Act

entries.description: Contingent Fee Exploitation and Illicit Commission Acceptance; a CPA or public accountancy firm soliciting, receiving, or paying a prohibited contingent fee or referral commission while performing attest services (such as audits, compilations, or examinations of prospective financial records) where professional independence is statutorily required.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Public Accountancy Act

entries.description: Unlicensed Practice of Architecture or Engineering; practicing, offering to practice, or using titles intended to deceive the public into believing the individual holds an active registration issued by the Board.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Architects, Engineers, Landscape Architects, and Interior Designers Act

entries.description: Professional Stamp/Seal Misuse; a registered architect, engineer, or landscape architect stamping, sealing, or signing plans, blueprints, or specifications that were not prepared by the registrant or under their direct, personal, and responsible supervisory control.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Architects, Engineers, Landscape Architects, and Interior Designers Act

entries.description: Professional Misconduct / Malpractice of Architects or Engineers; engaging in gross negligence, incompetence, or misconduct in the practice of architecture or engineering, practicing on an expired license, or violating the Board's rules of professional conduct.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Architects, Engineers, Landscape Architects, and Interior Designers Act

entries.description: Unlicensed Real Estate Brokerage; acting as a real estate broker, affiliate broker, timeshare salesperson, or property manager, or advertising or holding oneself out as such, without first obtaining an active license from the Tennessee Real Estate Commission.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Real Estate Broker License Act

entries.description: Unlicensed Real Estate Commission Treble Penalty; receiving money, compensation, or a commission as a consequence of acting as an unlicensed broker or salesperson, triggering an automatic civil penalty in a court of competent jurisdiction.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Real Estate Broker License Act

entries.description: Professional Broker Misconduct / License Violations; making material misrepresentations, pursuing a continued course of misrepresentation through affiliate brokers, failing to timely remit escrow or trust funds, or commingling personal funds with client real estate deposits.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Real Estate Broker License Act

entries.description: Escrow Account Commingling and Trust Account Defiance; failure of a principal broker to maintain a separate, distinct, and audited escrow or trust account for earnest money deposits, or willfully commingling client real estate trust funds with personal or corporate operational accounts.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Real Estate Broker License Act

entries.description: Real Estate Equity Theft and Deceptive Conveyance; a licensed or unlicensed real estate actor utilizing deceptive trade representations, straw buyers, or falsified closing summaries to execute fraudulent equity skimming, unauthorized title conversion, or theft of consumer earnest property.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Real Estate Broker License Act Integration

entries.description: Unlicensed Commercial or Residential Contracting; undertaking, bidding, or offering to undertake the construction, alteration, or repair of any building, highway, or structure where the total project value equals or exceeds $25,000 ($3,000 for masonry; $25,000 for roofing/HVAC) without holding an active contractor's license issued by the Board.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / State Contractors Licensing Act

entries.description: Contractor Civil Fraud and Material Misrepresentation; knowingly providing false financial state metrics, forging bonding documentation, or executing fraudulent sworn statements regarding lien waivers or subcontractor payouts to the Board, financial institutions, or consumers to secure project funds or certification.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Contractors Licensing Act Integration

entries.description: Professional Contractor Misconduct / Code Violations; engaging in gross negligence, financial structural abandonment of a project, bidding outside authorized monetary classification limits, or violating local building code rules resulting in active public safety hazards.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / State Contractors Licensing Act

entries.description: Unpermitted Combat Sports Promotion; promoting, staging, conducting, or participating in any professional boxing, sparring, martial arts, or mixed martial arts (MMA) match or public exhibition without securing an active promoter license or event permit from the Tennessee Athletic Commission.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / State Anti-Boxing and Combat Sports Regulation Act

entries.description: Conducting Unpermitted Combat Sports Event; promoting, staging, or conducting a professional boxing, sparring, martial arts, or mixed martial arts (MMA) event without securing an active, valid event permit and matching municipal bond clearance sheets from the Tennessee Athletic Commission.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Anti-Boxing and Combat Sports Regulation Act

entries.description: Athletic Commission Civil Non-Compliance; violating mandatory fighter medical clearance rules, failing to provide required ringside physician structures, or exceeding approved spectator limits at a combat sports exhibition.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Anti-Boxing and Combat Sports Regulation Act

entries.description: Unlicensed Auctioneer Promotion; calling, crying, guiding, or bidding at a public asset liquidation, real estate auction, or commercial auto sale, or advertising an auction, without first holding a valid active license from the Tennessee Auctioneer Commission.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Auctioneer License Act

entries.description: Unlicensed Barbering Operations; practicing the occupation of barbering, cutting or shaving hair for compensation, or operating a commercial barber shop facility without first holding an active certificate of registration from the Tennessee Board of Barber Examiners.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Barber Examiners Licensing Act

entries.description: Unlicensed Private Security Operations; acting as an armed or unarmed security guard, patrol service operator, or proprietary security organization without holding an active registration card or contract company license issued by the Commissioner of Commerce and Insurance.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Private Protective Services Licensing Act

entries.description: Unlawful Armed Guard Posting; working as an armed guard or posting armed security personnel who do not possess a valid firearm registration card or specific calibre endorsement, or carrying an unauthorized or non-registered type of weapon while on active contractual duty.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Private Protective Services Licensing Act

entries.description: Unlicensed Private Investigation; engaging in the business of private investigation, conducting commercial surveillance, or tracking individuals for hire without holding an active investigator license or company agency manager license.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Private Investigators Licensing and Regulatory Act

entries.description: Investigative Ethics Violation and Surveillance Abuse; a licensed investigator utilizing illegal wiretaps, hidden tracking bugs without owner consent, or committing fraud, extortion, or deliberate material misrepresentation while executing assignment monitoring.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Private Investigators Licensing and Regulatory Act

entries.description: Unlicensed Locksmith Operations; practicing locksmithing, marketing lock re-keying services, or replicating restricted master key codes without an active license from the Locksmith Licensing Program.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Locksmith Licensing Act of 2006

entries.description: Unlicensed Real Estate Appraisal; performing real estate appraisals, executing commercial evaluation charts, or issuing certified property valuations for banking lending purposes without holding an active registration or certification from the Real Estate Appraiser Commission.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / State Certified Real Estate Appraiser Act

entries.description: Real Estate Appraisal Falsification / Certified Misconduct; knowingly developing, signing, or certifying a fraudulent real estate appraisal report containing material misrepresentations, or manipulating market data comparisons to artificially inflate or deflate real property values for mortgage financing exploitation.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / State Licensed and Certified Real Estate Appraisers Law

entries.description: Appraiser Regulatory Violation / USPAP Non-Compliance; failing to adhere to the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice (USPAP), practicing on an expired or inactive registration, or failing to maintain required workfiles for state review.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / State Licensed and Certified Real Estate Appraisers Law

entries.description: Scrap Metal Dealer Record Violations / Purchasing Stolen Copper; failing to log required identification, fingerprints, or vehicle data from sellers, or purchasing regulated metals (such as copper wire, catalytic converters, or municipal grates) with reason to believe the items are stolen or illegally converted property.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Scrap Metal Dealers Registration Act

entries.description: Unlicensed Practice of Soil Science / Fraudulent Survey Reporting; practicing as a soil scientist, certifying environmental impact maps, or using the title "Licensed Professional Soil Scientist" without a valid active license from the Department, or knowingly falsifying soil absorption or compaction metrics to clear real estate zoning hurdles.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Professional Soil Scientists Licensing Act

entries.description: Purchasing Stolen Critical Infrastructure Scrap; a registered dealer knowingly or recklessly buying stolen HVAC copper coils, municipal water meters, electrical grid cabling, or highway markers, bypassing recording bars to conceal the illicit origin of property.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Penal Code / Scrap Metal Dealers Integration

entries.description: Unlicensed Practice of Soil Science; practicing, offering to practice, or utilizing the title "Licensed Professional Soil Scientist" on municipal environmental health documents or agricultural drainage plans without an active certificate issued by the Department of Commerce and Insurance.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Professional Soil Scientists Licensing Act

entries.description: Unlicensed Practice of Medicine; practicing medicine, surgery, or obstetrics, diagnosing ailments, prescribing pharmaceuticals, or utilizing titles intended to deceive the public into believing the individual holds an active license from the Tennessee Board of Medical Examiners.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Medicine and Surgery Regulation

entries.description: Unlicensed Practice of Podiatric Medicine; practicing podiatry, performing unapproved foot/ankle surgical maneuvers, or holding oneself out as a podiatrist (DPM) without a valid license from the Tennessee Board of Podiatric Medical Examiners.

Unlawful Corporate Practice of Podiatry; a corporation, partnership, or limited liability company (LLC) offering or rendering professional podiatric services or ankle surgeries without a designated registered practitioner in responsible charge, or failing to maintain a valid facility disclosure listing with the Board.

Unlicensed Practice of Veterinary Medicine; practicing veterinary medicine, surgery, or dentistry on animals, diagnosing animal conditions, prescribing veterinary drugs, or using matching titles without an active license from the Board.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Veterinary Practice Act

entries.description: Unlawful Veterinary Narcotics Dispensing; an uncertified veterinary assistant or technician compounding, dispensing, or executing automated delivery of controlled substances or dangerous animal pharmaceuticals without the explicit direct supervision of a licensed veterinarian.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Veterinary Practice Act

entries.description: Professional Veterinary Misconduct / Ketamine-Euthanasia Diversion; engaging in chronic substance dependency, diverting veterinary narcotics (such as ketamine, tramadol, or euthanasia solutions) to human use, or executing gross malpractice leading to unnecessary animal destruction or torture.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Veterinary Practice Act

entries.description: Professional Therapy Misconduct / Patient Boundary Violation; violating therapist-client sexual or financial boundaries, exploiting vulnerable psychiatric clients, executing deceptive billing practices under corporate therapy networks, or being convicted of standard felonies.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Professional Counselors, Marital and Family Therapists, and Clinical Pastoral Therapists Act

entries.description: Unlicensed Social Work Practice; practicing, offering to practice, or using titles such as "Licensed Clinical Social Worker," "LCSW," or "Licensed Master Social Worker" without holding an active certificate or license issued by the Board.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Social Workers Practice Act

entries.description: Social Work Professional Misconduct / Patient Exploitation; engaging in unprofessional conduct, gross negligence, boundary violations, or exploiting a clinically vulnerable patient for personal, emotional, or financial gain while licensed as a social worker.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Social Workers Practice Act

entries.description: Nursing Home Administrator Misconduct / Facility Neglect; engaging in gross negligence, financial malfeasance, fraud, or violating safe facility staffing rules, causing direct patient care endangerment, systemic neglect, or institutional abuse conditions within a licensed long-term care facility.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Nursing Home Administrators Licensing Act

entries.description: Unlicensed Practice as a Nursing Home Administrator; practicing, acting, or holding oneself out as a nursing home administrator, or managing a long-term care facility without an active, valid license issued by the Board of Examiners for Nursing Home Administrators.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Nursing Home Administrators Licensing Act

entries.description: Unlicensed Mental Health Practice; advertising, offering, or providing professional counseling, marital and family therapy, or utilizing protected titles (LPC, LMFT) without holding an active registration from the state board.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Professional Counselors and Therapists Act

entries.description: Willful Attempt to Evade or Defeat State Taxes; willfully attempting in any manner to evade or defeat any tax imposed by the Tennessee Revenue Title, or filing a false or fraudulent state tax return (Sales, Franchise, Excise, or Mixed Drink taxes).

Criminal Usury; collecting, contracting for, or receiving interest at a rate that exceeds the maximum statutory formula rates willfully and knowingly, typically run through unlicensed lending, cash syndicates, or predatory operations.

Willful Failure to Perform Duty under Unclaimed Property Act; willfully failing to report, pay, or deliver abandoned assets to the Tennessee State Treasurer after the legal statutory dormancy timeframe expires.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Uniform Unclaimed Property Act

entries.description: Willful Violations of Consumer Credit / Financing Disclosures; willfully making false statements or failing to provide required financing disclosures regarding rates, loan parameters, or fees, or failing to comply with rules under the Tennessee Industrial Loan and Thrift framework.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Industrial Loan and Thrift Companies Act

entries.description: Extortion; knowingly utilizing an implicit or explicit threat to cause bodily injury, property damage, reputational harm, or dynamic economic leverage to compel another person to hand over money, goods, or services.

Failure to Collect, Account For, or Pay Over Trust Taxes; willfully failing to collect, truthfully account for, and pay over state trust taxes (such as retail sales tax or employee withholding taxes).

Money Laundering; knowingly conducting, supervising, or facilitating a financial transaction involving property or currency derived from specified criminal activities with the intent to promote the criminal enterprise or conceal the nature, location, source, ownership, or control of the proceeds.

Willful Failure to File Return or Supply Information; willfully failing to pay an estimated tax, make a return, keep required commercial records, or supply tracking information to the Department of Revenue at the time required by law.

Willful Making of False Statement Under Perjury Penalties; willfully making and signing any state tax return, report, statement, or other document verified by a written declaration that it is made under the penalties of perjury, which the maker does not believe to be true and correct as to every material matter.

Forfeiture of Usurious Interest and Contract Invalidation; entering into a lending arrangement that charges interest exceeding the maximum statutory limits, triggering mandatory civil forfeiture of all excess interest and providing the debtor the right to void the underlying loan contract entirely.

Compounding Administrative Penalties and Interest for Unclaimed Property Non-Compliance; the structured civil mechanism utilized by the State Treasurer to enforce compliance against corporate holders who fail to report, pay, or deliver abandoned property within the statutory timeframe.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Uniform Unclaimed Property Act

entries.description: Issuance and Execution of Arrest Warrant; the formal judicial process where a magistrate or judge finds probable cause via written affidavit that a financial felony has occurred, authorizing law enforcement to seize the person and transport them to a secure intake facility.

Criminal Summons in Lieu of Warrant; the statutory procedural mandate requiring judges or clerks to issue a written summons instead of a physical arrest warrant for non-felony violations, ordering the individual to appear for booking and arraignment on a set date without custodial capture.

Seizure Warrant / Forfeiture Order Requirement; the mandatory judicial process requiring law enforcement to obtain a forfeiture warrant from a judge within five business days of seizing any cash, bank accounts, vehicles, or ledger hardware linked to a money laundering or usury syndicate.

Notice of Charge and Booking Restrictions / Telephone Rule; the mandatory statutory restriction prohibiting booking desk personnel from listing an arrestee's name in any public log until the individual has been given one hour to successfully complete a phone call to an attorney, family member, or bond agent.

Mandatory Inmate Intake Record Keeping; the administrative regulation governing all local county jails across Tennessee, forcing the booking clerk to immediately collect and lock explicit metadata into the local system: mugshot, booking number, exact numeric statutory charge code, automated finger scan, and warrant source data.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Rules and Regulations / Tennessee Corrections Institute (TCI)

entries.description: Use of Citations in Lieu of Continued Custodial Arrest; the operational framework governing when an officer can bypass physical jail transport for misdemeanors.

Engaging in Unlicensed Money Transmission; willfully engaging in the business of money transmission, running cash operations, or cash-skimming syndicates without obtaining an explicit operating license from the Tennessee Department of Financial Institutions.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Tennessee Money Transmitter Act

entries.description: Using False Exemption Certificates or Operating Without a Resale Certificate; using a faked, altered, or fraudulent resale certificate to buy commercial goods without paying retail sales tax, or operating a retail establishment without displaying a current, valid certificate of registration from the Department of Revenue.
Aggravated Rape; unlawful sexual penetration accompanied by force, weapon, or causing bodily injury.
Rape; unlawful sexual penetration without consent via force, coercion, or intoxication.
Sexual Battery; unlawful sexual contact with a victim via force, coercion, or lack of consent.
Violation of the Tennessee Sex Offender Registry Act; failure to register, verify address, or reporting false information.
Unlawful photography; capturing images of an individual's private areas without consent, now requiring mandatory registration.
Criminal Arrest Warrant issued by a magistrate or judge for an underlying sex offense charge.
entries.statuteTitle: Tennessee Code Annotated (T.C.A.) / Rules of Criminal Procedure

entries.description: Civil Order of Protection issued based on a judicial finding of sexual assault or abuse.
Aggravated Rape of a Child (Adult Offender); unlawful sexual penetration of a victim by an adult defendant (or vice versa) where the victim is eight (8) years of age or less.
Aggravated Rape of a Child (Juvenile Offender); unlawful sexual penetration of a victim eight (8) years of age or less, where the perpetrator is legally a juvenile.

Especially Aggravated Rape of a Child (Adult Offender); unlawful sexual penetration of a child victim accompanied by three or more statutory aggravating factors (e.g., torture, mutilation, kidnapping).
Aggravated Sexual Battery; unlawful sexual contact (touching or fondling) with a child victim who is less than thirteen (13) years of age.

Continuous Sexual Abuse of a Child (Class A); engaging in three (3) or more separate acts of sexual abuse against a child victim over a duration of time, where the underlying acts include Aggravated Rape of a Child or Rape of a Child.
Accessory after the fact; intentionally harboring, concealing, providing means of escape, or warning a known felon to hinder arrest, trial, conviction, or punishment.
Aggravated prostitution; engaging in sexual activity as a business, or acting as an inmate in a house of prostitution while knowing oneself to be infected with HIV.
Aggravated rape of a child; unlawful sexual penetration of a child victim three (3) years of age or less.
Aggravated sexual battery; unlawful sexual contact accompanied by force, weapons, injury, or where the victim is less than thirteen (13) years of age.

Aggravated sexual exploitation of a minor; knowingly promoting, selling, distributing, transporting, or purchasing material that includes a minor engaged in sexual activity or simulated sexual activity.

Aggravated Spousal Rape; unlawful sexual penetration of one spouse by the other, accompanied by especially cruel, vile, and inhumane conduct, where the defendant is armed with a weapon or causes serious bodily injury.
Assault with intent to commit or attempt to commit rape; an assaultive act executed with the specific concurrent intent to accomplish unlawful sexual penetration.
Assault with intent to commit or attempt to commit sexual battery; an assault executed with the explicit intent to accomplish unlawful sexual contact (touching or fondling).
Criminal attempt; intentionally engaging in an action or taking a substantial step that would constitute a criminal offense if circumstances were as believed.
Criminal conspiracy; an agreement between two or more people to engage in conduct constituting an offense, accompanied by an overt act in pursuance of the agreement.
Criminal exposure to HIV; knowingly engaging in intimate contact with another individual while knowing oneself to be infected with HIV.

Criminal sexual conduct in the first degree; engaging in sexual penetration with a victim who is twelve (12) years of age or under, or less than sixteen (16) years of age if the actor is a relative or holds custodial/official authority.

Criminal sexual conduct in the second degree; engaging in unlawful sexual contact with a victim under thirteen (13) years of age, or a victim between 13 and 16 years of age under blood relation or custodial authority.

Criminal sexual conduct in the third degree; engaging in sexual penetration with a victim aged 13 to 16 where the actor is at least 3 years older, or utilizing force/coercion, or under the pretext of a medical examination.

Especially aggravated kidnapping; false imprisonment accomplished with a deadly weapon, where the victim was under thirteen (13) years of age, or held for ransom/hostage, or suffers serious bodily injury.

Especially Aggravated Sexual Exploitation of a Minor; knowingly promoting, employing, using, assisting, transporting, or permitting a minor to participate in the performance or production of obscene sexual materials.

Soliciting Sexual Exploitation of a Minor / Electronic Exploitation; an adult intentionally commanding, inducing, or causing a minor via electronic, oral, or written means to engage in sexual or simulated sexual activity observed by the person.

Electronic Solicitation / Forced Exposure to a Minor; intentionally displaying or exposing a minor to obscene or patently offensive sexual materials for sexual gratification, or doing so to a law enforcement officer posing as a minor.
Incest; knowingly engaging in sexual penetration with a closely related individual (e.g., parent, child, grandparent, sibling, stepchild, nephew, niece).
Indecent exposure; intentionally exposing genitals/buttocks or engaging in sexual acts publicly, or enticing a child under 13 into a residence for masturbation/exposure.
Kidnapping; false imprisonment under circumstances exposing the victim to a substantial risk of bodily injury, where the victim is a minor and offender is a non-parent.

Promotion of performances including sexual conduct by children; knowingly producing, directing, manufacturing, or selling a performance (play, film, photo, dance) that features actual or simulated sexual conduct by a minor.

Sexual Battery by an Authority Figure; unlawful sexual contact with a victim aged 13 to 17 (or helpless at any age) where the actor leverages a position of trust, supervisory/disciplinary power, or parental/custodial authority.
Solicitation; intentionally commanding, requesting, or hiring another person to commit a criminal offense, with the intent that the offense be committed.

Solicitation of a Minor; an adult intentionally commanding, requesting, or attempting to induce a minor (or law enforcement officer posing as a minor) to engage in specified severe sexual felonies (e.g., child rape, battery, exploitation).
Spousal Rape (Aggravated Variant); unlawful sexual penetration of one spouse by the other where the defendant is armed with a weapon or causes serious bodily injury.
Spousal Rape (Separated Variant); unlawful sexual penetration of one spouse by the other where the spouses are living apart and one has filed for separate maintenance or divorce.
Spousal Sexual Battery (Aggravated Variant); unlawful sexual contact (touching/fondling) of one spouse by the other where the defendant is armed with a weapon or causes serious bodily injury.
Spousal Sexual Battery (Separated Variant); unlawful sexual contact of one spouse by the other where the spouses are living apart and one has filed for separate maintenance or divorce.

Statutory Rape; unlawful sexual penetration of a minor victim aged 13 to 17 where a specified age differential exists between the victim and defendant (e.g., defendant 4–5 years older for mitigated tracks; 4–9 years older for standard minor brackets).

Aggravated Statutory Rape; unlawful sexual penetration of a victim who is at least thirteen (13) but less than eighteen (18) years of age, where the defendant is at least ten (10) years older than the victim.
Especially Aggravated Rape of a Child; unlawful sexual penetration of a child under 13 accompanied by severe aggravating factors such as torture, serious injury, or use of a deadly weapon.
Prostitution; engaging in sexual activity as a business for hire. (Note: Victims under 18 are immune from prosecution and diverted to services).

Patronizing Prostitution; soliciting or hiring another person with the intent to engage in prostitution, or entering/remaining in a brothel. (Note: If the person patronized is under 18, it is punished under severe human trafficking laws).
Promoting Prostitution; owning, managing, or supervising a prostitution business/house, or procuring inmates/prostitutes for patrons. (Excludes self-promotion).

Continuous Sexual Abuse of a Child (Class B Variant); engaging in multiple acts of sexual abuse against a child victim over a period where exactly two of the acts constitute the severe offenses listed in subdivision (c)(1).
Prostitution within Prohibited Zone; engaging in prostitution within 100 feet of a church or within 1.5 miles of a school.

Trafficking for a Commercial Sex Act (Minor Victim); knowingly recruiting, enticing, harboring, or obtaining a child under eighteen (18) years of age for the purpose of a commercial sex act. (Note: Force or coercion is not required to prove this offense if the victim is a minor).

Indecent Exposure (Residential Minor Enticement); an adult (18+) knowingly inviting, enticing, or fraudulently inducing a minor aged 13 to 17 into a private residence to expose genitals/buttocks or masturbate without consent for sexual gratification.

Possession or Production of Technology/AI Tools for Child Exploitation; knowingly possessing, distributing, or producing software, technology, or digital tools designed or modified by artificial intelligence for the purpose of creating material depicting a minor engaged in sexual activity.
Violation of Community Supervision for Life; knowingly violating a restriction or condition of lifetime supervision (e.g., entering a school zone, failing a polygraph, tracking device manipulation).

Child Abuse and Neglect (Baseline); knowingly, other than by accidental means, treating a child under eighteen (18) years of age in such a manner as to inflict injury or neglect affecting the child's health and welfare.
Child Abuse and Neglect (Felony Grade); knowingly treating or neglecting a child where the victim is eight (8) years of age or less.

Aggravated Child Abuse / Aggravated Child Neglect; committing child abuse or neglect as defined in § 39-15-401 that results in serious bodily injury, or involves the use of a deadly weapon, or where the child is eight (8) years of age or less and suffers serious injury.
Criminal Non-Support; a person who intentionally fails to provide support to their minor child or child over 18 who is disabled, when able to provide support.

entries.subcategory: Contributing to Delinquency of a Minor
entries.description: Contributing to Delinquency, Unruly Conduct, or Dependency; any adult who, by any act or omission, intentionally contributes to, encourages, or tends to cause any child to become unruly, delinquent, or dependent and neglected.

Elder / Vulnerable Adult Abuse (Knowing Abuse); knowingly abusing an adult who is eighteen (18) years of age or older and who is unable to manage their own resources, carry out activities of daily living, or protect themselves from neglect, harm, or exploitation due to mental or physical dysfunction.
